IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: Heroic low-angle finale shot. CAMERA MOVEMENT: one move only — a slow push-in and slight rise settling on Aira standing. LENS: 35mm-equivalent, f/4. PHYSICS: the sled decelerates and settles on its suspension with a final rock; Aira rises against residual momentum; the last crystals lose suspension and chime down, fading gently; spindrift settles. SUBJECT + OBJECT ACTION (timecoded): 0.0–2.0s the sled slews to a stop on the overlook, suspension rocking and settling (spindrift drifting forward); 2.0–4.0s Aira pushes up to stand on the boards and lifts the helmet off, braid loosening; 4.0–6.0s she exhales a long frost-breath and a brief fierce smile breaks as the last crystals chime and fade around her. EXPRESSION TRANSITION: into into — hard focus melts to a heaving-chest release at 2.8s, resolving into eased-brow triumph and a brief fierce smile by 5.2s. LIGHTING: aurora key, cyan rim, crystal practicals. ENVIRONMENT: glacial overlook, endless ice, aurora. AUDIO (Block A): the engine spooling down to an idle then off, a tick of cooling metal, a high wind, a long frost-breath exhale at 2.8s, a final crystalline shimmer as the crystals fade; score lands a choir-and-taiko stinger then tails to the opening wind for the loop. DURATION: 6.0s. FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180°, gentle blur on the rise, crisp on the face. LIP-SYNC: none; an exhale and a closed-lip smile. NEGATIVE: no cut, no second move, no extra people, no warped geometry, no text or watermark.

---

## Concept 06 — Avalanche Ski-Chase Combat

Lane: Snow + unarmed combat · a downhill ski-chase fought hand-to-hand at speed (flying kicks, spinning kicks, punches, throws).

Format: Seven-frame vertical action reel — one continuous avalanche-chased downhill ski run with a running unarmed fight rendered as seven seeded keyframes, roughly twenty-one seconds.

Combat Note: No weapons. Aira fights a masked pursuer entirely with martial-arts strikes — a sliding low kick, a mid-air flying side-kick, a spinning back-kick, snapping punches, a wrist-throw — all stylized, non-gory; impacts land on the pursuer's guard and gear and on the environment, never as injury detail.

Pursuer: A masked downhill pursuer in matte-black thermal armour, skiing and striking back; takes stylized hits, tumbles into powder, never gory.

Setting: A vast, steep alpine face at dawn, deep untracked powder, rock bands and a couloir, a colossal avalanche breaking loose above and chasing the skiers down, peaks glowing pink with alpenglow. Every zone of the frame intentionally designed, immaculately clean and premium, with layered foreground, midground and background depth and rich environmental storytelling. Sky richly graded and cinematic with elegant clouds and premium light, never flat or empty.

Wardrobe: A fitted high-performance ski suit in white with electric-coral piping, articulated armour panels, high collar — full-coverage, modest, freeride-coded.

Footwear: White freeride ski boots with coral buckles locked into twin-tip skis, reinforced cuff.

Eye Colour: Warm amber-brown (consistent locked identity), catching alpenglow-pink, fixed downhill.

Accessories: A white ski helmet with a mirrored rose-gold goggle visor, armoured gloves, a slim airbag pack, the thin titanium wrist cuff; ski poles used for balance, never as weapons.

Hair: The same tight low braid, tucked into the collar, tail flicking free.

Makeup: Matte cold-flushed complexion, defined brow, cool eye, neutral lip; frost-breath and sweat-sheen permitted.

Movement (the one impossible move): At the peak of the chase, the towering powder cloud of the avalanche gathers into a vast, benevolent snow-spirit — a luminous figure of swirling snow that sweeps protectively alongside Aira, then disperses into glittering powder.

Concept Line: "The mountain comes apart behind her — so she fights, and flies, downhill."

Duration: ~21 seconds · 7 frames · 24fps · 180° shutter.

Mood: Breathless, vast, heroic, luminous.

Composition: Steep downhill verticals, avalanche wall behind, powder-spray foregrounds, the skiers raked across thirds; intercut a low chase plate, a mid-air fight two-shot, a flying-kick hero beat, and a hero stop. Rich layered foreground-to-background depth, elegant spatial hierarchy and clean visual flow (original framing and angle preserved).

Grade: Cold dawn blue-white, alpenglow pink-coral accents, luminous white for the snow-spirit, rich blacks, controlled bloom on powder and sky. World-class editorial colour — rich and vivid yet clean, luminous mid-tones, deep-but-clean blacks, controlled saturation, nothing washed-out, natural skin protected.

Identity Safety: Aira rendered entirely from the locked reference sheet; adult, 25; no physical or ethnic enumeration; full-coverage wardrobe; stylized non-gory combat, impacts on guard, gear and environment only.

### 🎚️ Master Soundtrack & Sound-Design Brief — Audio Block B (outside the prompts)

Role: The continuous score and sound-design bed under the in-frame diegetic audio across all seven frames, binding them into one twenty-one-second piece. Genre & palette: Heroic hybrid-orchestral with martial percussion — surging strings, war-taiko and tight fight-percussion, a low brass avalanche-motif, a luminous wordless soprano reserved for the snow-spirit, and wind-and-snow ambience. Tempo & key: 138 BPM, D minor, 4/4, locked so strikes and impacts land on the downbeat. Instrumentation by layer: sub and brass (avalanche power), war-taiko and tight percussion (the fight strikes), surging strings (the chase), luminous soprano and bells (the snow-spirit magic), granular wind-and-powder texture (atmosphere). Dynamic arc: Frame 1 sparse and tense (wind and low brass only); Frame 2 brass-and-taiko launch as the avalanche breaks; Frames 3–5 fight-percussion drives the running combat; Frame 6 the soprano snow-spirit motif blooms over a riser; Frame 7 a triumphant final hit tailing to the opening wind for a perfect loop. Hits & transitions: a brass avalanche-boom on the break (F2), tight percussion accents synced to each strike (F3–F5), a riser into the flying-kick (F5), a beat-dropout and 250ms suspended silence as the snow-spirit forms (F5→F6), a taiko-and-soprano stinger on the hero stop (F7). Mix: score about 5 dB under fight-impacts and the avalanche roar; sidechain the bed to the biggest hits; wide-stereo strings and soprano, mono-tight sub; tape saturation, alpine reverb on the soprano. Loop point: the closing wind matches bar one.

MASTER AUDIO: a 42-second custom cinematic score (7 clips × 6s) composed in self-resolving ~6-second phrases, so it can be trimmed to the reel’s assembled length at any phrase boundary without a hard cut — it always resolves, never stops dead or loops audibly. Built on deep trailer percussion, a driving synth bass and brass swells — premium, cinematic and emotionally catching, never generic stock or empty ambience. It opens immediately on a low brass-and-sub impact (no fade-in) so the mood lands within the first second. Emotional arc: an airy intimate open across the first beats, a rising shimmer of anticipation toward frame 5, the warm emotional peak on the signature move — At the peak of the chase, the towering powder cloud of the avalanche gathers into a…, then a graceful resolve back toward the opening for an invisible loop. SPOKEN reel — the score opens space for the spoken line at its beat and ducks gently beneath the voice, then re-blooms after. Layered as one system — lead melody, warm sub for body, taut percussive tension as atmosphere, restrained percussion, and event impacts reserved for the major beats, each tuned to the score’s own key so every hit reads as the music itself expanding, never an added sound effect.

Her body is alive and committed to the motion, in a three-quarter view to camera. Camera distance follows the shot size, holding the action sharp. The beat: the magic blooms — the towering avalanche powder-cloud gathers into a vast, benevolent snow-spirit, a luminous figure of swirling snow that sweeps protectively alongside Aira as she lands and skis on, the pursuer left behind in the powder. Her pose reads as weight settling from the landing, chest lifting, shoulders opening with exhilaration — gaze level and reading the scene with cold focus; head steady, turned to the action; both shoulders settled and squared, alive with breath; the RIGHT arm poised and ready with the hand relaxed but set; the LEFT arm easy and counter-balanced at her side; torso upright and composed in living stillness; waist long with the hips squared, weight carried on the back foot; the RIGHT leg bearing the load with a soft knee while the LEFT braces with one heel just lifted; both feet planted and grounded, weight back and ready to spring; hair alive in the wind, sealed and controlled. ORIENTATION LOCK — preserve this exact left-to-right composition; do not mirror, flip or invert the frame. LENS + DOF + BOKEH: 85mm-equivalent, f/2.8, spirit in luminous bokeh, sharp on her face. ATMOSPHERE: suspended glowing powder, drifting light-motes, the spirit's swirling form. COLOR GRADE: luminous white and alpenglow pink-coral, deep cold-blue shadow for contrast, gentle bloom on the spirit. TEXTURE & MICRO-DETAIL: skin, fine grain in the snow-spirit's swirl, light-glints, powder on the suit catching glow. NEGATIVE: no gore, no weapons, no extra people up close, no warped face, no garish clipping, no logos, no oversaturation beyond intended bloom, no sexualization, no enumeration, no text.

### Frame 6 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT
STRICT COLOUR & RENDER MANDATE (must be followed exactly; this governs render QUALITY only and must NOT change the composition, camera, lens, framing, wardrobe, action or identity specified above): render the colours already specified at their richest, freshest, cleanest and most premium — crisp, vivid and luminous with elegant, controlled saturation; never dull, faded, washed-out, muddy, grey or flat. Hold deep-but-clean blacks, luminous full mid-tones and beautiful highlight roll-off with high dynamic range and no blown highlights. Wherever sky is visible, render it richly graded and cinematic — never empty, white or overexposed. Wherever water is visible, render it crystal-clear with deep sea-blue and turquoise gradients, clean caustics and sparkling reflections — never grey or murky (unless the concept explicitly specifies dark / ink / obsidian water, which stays deep, glassy and clean). Protect natural, true skin tone; never push saturation onto the face. Keep the image immaculately clean and premium with clear figure-to-ground separation so the hero reads first; honour the concept's intended dominant surface and full-frame coverage, and do NOT reveal unwanted structural edges, seams, side-walls or empty dead space that break the intended composition. Photoreal, award-winning editorial finish — no AI artifacts, plastic or waxy skin, dead eyes, malformed hands or unfinished backgrounds.

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: Hero three-quarter reveal shot. CAMERA MOVEMENT: one move only — a slow rise-and-settle (crane up) revealing the snow-spirit sweeping beside her. LENS: 85mm-equivalent, f/2.8. PHYSICS: Aira lands and the skis plane onto the powder; the avalanche cloud, instead of consuming, gathers and organizes into a luminous humanoid swirl of snow that holds cohesion, sweeps alongside with protective grace, then sheds slow glittering motes; light refracts through the swirling powder. SUBJECT + OBJECT ACTION (timecoded): 0.0–2.0s Aira lands and skis on, the avalanche powder-cloud slowing and brightening (snow organizing in the air); 2.0–4.0s the cloud gathers into a vast luminous snow-spirit that sweeps protectively alongside her, motes drifting; 4.0–6.0s she skis on beside the spirit down the fall-line, the figure trailing glittering powder. EXPRESSION TRANSITION: into into — strained effort releases into parted-lip awe at 2.4s as the spirit forms, blooming into a wild grin by 4.8s. LIGHTING: self-luminous snow-spirit on her face, glowing rim. ENVIRONMENT: open slope transformed as the avalanche becomes a guardian of light. AUDIO (Block A): the avalanche roar softening into a hushed shimmer, a luminous chime as the spirit forms at 2.0s, a soft awed exhale-laugh at 4.8s; the wordless soprano snow-spirit motif blooms over a riser. DURATION: 6.0s. FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180°, silky blur on the rise, crisp spirit. LIP-SYNC: none; a breath of wonder. NEGATIVE: no gore, no weapons, no cut, no second move, no cartoonish figure, no text.

## Concept 07 — Frozen-Lake Snowcat Demolition

Lane: Snow vehicle · armored snowcat demolition duel on cracking lake ice.

Format: Seven-frame vertical action reel — one continuous armored-snowcat duel across a vast frozen lake at dusk rendered as seven seeded keyframes, roughly twenty-one seconds.

Hero Vehicle: A heavy armored snowcat (tracked piste machine) in gunmetal-and-ice-white with amber strobes, reinforced blade-bumper, caged cab, twin tracks; progressive realistic damage — dented blade, cracked light bar, ice-scarred flanks — never disabled, always grinding forward.

Rival Vehicle: A rust-red armored snowcat with a battering ram, trading heavy hits; takes its own dents and never explodes.

Setting: A colossal frozen lake at dusk, black ice veined with white stress-fractures, pressure ridges, snow-dusted islets, a violet-and-amber dusk sky, the ice booming and splitting under the weight. Every zone of the frame intentionally designed, immaculately clean and premium, with layered foreground, midground and background depth and rich environmental storytelling. Water kept deep, clean and glassy — premium and clear, never muddy or grey. Sky richly graded and cinematic with elegant clouds and premium light, never flat or empty.

Wardrobe: A fitted insulated mechanic-style snow suit in gunmetal with amber piping, padded shoulders, high collar — full-coverage, modest, utility-coded.

Footwear: Heavy insulated work-boots in black with amber laces, steel-reinforced toe, deep lugs.

Eye Colour: Warm amber-brown (consistent locked identity), catching amber strobe-flash, fixed on the rival.

Accessories: A gunmetal crash-helmet with a clear visor (worn from Frame 2), heavy work-gauntlets, a thin titanium wrist cuff, a harness in the caged cab.

Hair: The same tight low braid, tucked into the collar, tail flicking free.

Makeup: Matte cold-flushed complexion, defined brow, cool eye, neutral lip; sweat and frost permitted.

Movement (the one impossible move): When a heavy ram cracks the ice, the spreading fracture does not simply break — it blooms into vast glowing fractal patterns of light that race across the black ice like luminous frostwork, then fade as the cat grinds on.

Concept Line: "She fights on breaking ice — and the cracks bloom into light."

Duration: ~21 seconds · 7 frames · 24fps · 180° shutter.

Mood: Heavy, crystalline, industrial, sublime.

Composition: Low wide ice horizons, fracture leading-lines, blade-bumper foregrounds, the cats raked across thirds; intercut a low chase plate, a ram two-shot, a power-pivot hero beat, and a hero stop. Premium foreground, midground and background layering with strong three-dimensional depth and clean visual balance (original framing preserved).

Grade: Black-ice blue, dusk violet-amber, cold strobe accents, glowing cyan-white for the fractal magic, rich blacks, controlled bloom on ice and lights. Premium editorial grade — rich clean colour, luminous mid-tones, deep-but-clean blacks, controlled elegant saturation, no faded or muddy tones, natural true skin preserved.

Identity Safety: Aira rendered entirely from the locked reference sheet; adult, 25; no physical or ethnic enumeration; full-coverage wardrobe; stylized stunt, damage on the cats and ice only.

### 🎚️ Master Soundtrack & Sound-Design Brief — Audio Block B (outside the prompts)

Role: The continuous score and sound-design bed under the in-frame diegetic audio across all seven frames, binding them into one twenty-one-second piece. Genre & palette: Industrial-cinematic — grinding metal percussion, a sub-heavy drone, a tense brass motif, a crystalline glass-and-bell theme reserved for the fractal magic, and ice-and-wind ambience. Tempo & key: 120 BPM, B-flat minor, 4/4, locked so rams and impacts land on the downbeat. Instrumentation by layer: sub and distorted bass (engine and mass), industrial metal-hits and taiko (the rams), tense brass (the duel), shimmering glass and bells (the fractal magic), granular ice-and-wind texture (atmosphere). Dynamic arc: Frame 1 sparse and groaning (drone and ice only); Frames 2–3 industrial percussion and bass enter with the launch and charge; Frame 4 a brass stab on the rival ram; Frame 5 a metal-hit riser into the power-pivot; Frame 6 the fractal glass-and-bell theme blooms over a riser; Frame 7 a triumphant final hit tailing to the opening drone for a perfect loop. Hits & transitions: sub-drop on the launch (F2), a massive metal-clang on the rival ram (F4), a grinding riser into the power-pivot (F5), a beat-dropout and 250ms suspended silence as the fractals bloom (F5→F6), a brass-and-glass stinger on the hero stop (F7). Mix: score about 6 dB under engine and metal-impacts; sidechain the bed to the biggest rams; wide-stereo brass and glass, mono-tight sub; tape saturation, vast lake-reverb on the bells. Loop point: the closing drone matches bar one.

MASTER AUDIO: a 42-second custom cinematic score (7 clips × 6s) composed in self-resolving ~6-second phrases, so it can be trimmed to the reel’s assembled length at any phrase boundary without a hard cut — it always resolves, never stops dead or loops audibly. Built on a delicate felt piano, atmospheric pads and elegant glass textures — premium, cinematic and emotionally catching, never generic stock or empty ambience. It opens immediately on a soft piano strike with a low sub-bloom (no fade-in) so the mood lands within the first second. Emotional arc: an airy intimate open across the first beats, a rising shimmer of anticipation toward frame 5, the warm emotional peak on the signature move — When a heavy ram cracks the ice, the spreading fracture does not simply break, then a graceful resolve back toward the opening for an invisible loop. SPOKEN reel — the score opens space for the spoken line at its beat and ducks gently beneath the voice, then re-blooms after. Layered as one system — lead melody, warm sub for body, elegant glassy shimmer as atmosphere, restrained percussion, and event impacts reserved for the major beats, each tuned to the score’s own key so every hit reads as the music itself expanding, never an added sound effect.

Wherever water is visible, render it crystal-clear with deep sea-blue and turquoise gradients, clean caustics and sparkling reflections — never grey or murky (unless the concept explicitly specifies dark / ink / obsidian water, which stays deep, glassy and clean). Protect natural, true skin tone; never push saturation onto the face. Keep the image immaculately clean and premium with clear figure-to-ground separation so the hero reads first; honour the concept's intended dominant surface and full-frame coverage, and do NOT reveal unwanted structural edges, seams, side-walls or empty dead space that break the intended composition. Photoreal, award-winning editorial finish — no AI artifacts, plastic or waxy skin, dead eyes, malformed hands or unfinished backgrounds.

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: Low side-on power-pivot hero shot. CAMERA MOVEMENT: one move only — an arcing orbit following the cat's rotation. LENS: 50mm-equivalent, f/2.8. PHYSICS: a counter-rotating skid-turn — one track locks as the pivot, the other drives, rotating the heavy mass through a controlled 180°; the raised blade sweeps around with the cat's angular momentum and shoves the rival's ram aside; a curtain of shattered ice is thrown by the spinning tracks; the rival is flung into a spin without breach. SUBJECT + OBJECT ACTION (timecoded): 0.0–1.6s Aira locks one track and powers the other, the cat beginning a hard pivot (ice-curtain erupting from the tracks); 1.6–3.6s the cat rotates through the skid, the blade sweeping around to meet the rival's ram; 3.6–4.6s the blade shoves the ram aside, flinging the rust-red cat into a spin; 4.6–6.0s the hero cat completes the pivot and gathers forward, ice-curtain towering and backlit. EXPRESSION TRANSITION: into — wide-eyed shock clamps into bared-teeth, neck-corded effort by 1.6s, held at maximum strain through the pivot. LIGHTING: dusk firing through the ice-curtain, glowing rim. ENVIRONMENT: ramming range, immense ice-curtain, rival spinning away. DURATION: 6.0s. FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180°, sweeping blur on ice, crisp cab. LIP-SYNC: none; a strained effort sound. NEGATIVE: no gore, no cab breach, no cut, no warped cat, no text.

## Concept 08 — Coastal Jet-Ski Cliff Run

Lane: Water vehicle · jet-ski run along towering sea cliffs.

Format: Eight-frame vertical action reel — one continuous high-speed jet-ski run along a turquoise cliff coast at golden hour rendered as eight seeded keyframes, roughly twenty-four seconds.

Hero Vehicle: A race jet-ski in pearl-white over turquoise with coral accents, deep-vee hull, sponsons, spray rails; progressive realistic damage — salt-scuffed hull, a chipped nose, a scraped sponson — never swamped, always skipping.

Setting: A dramatic coastline of towering ochre sea-cliffs at golden hour, turquoise water shading to deep blue, rock arches and sea caves, a big rolling swell, spray hanging in the warm light, gulls wheeling. Every zone of the frame intentionally designed, immaculately clean and premium, with layered foreground, midground and background depth and rich environmental storytelling. Water rendered crystal-clear in rich sea-blue and turquoise gradients, with beautiful caustics and clean sparkling reflections. Architecture in fine premium materials with an immaculate finish.

Wardrobe: A fitted coral-and-white wetsuit-style race suit with reflective piping, articulated panels, high collar — full-coverage, modest, watersport-coded.

Footwear: White neoprene race boots with coral soles, ankle-snug, grippy for the foot-wells.

Eye Colour: Warm amber-brown (consistent locked identity), catching gold-on-turquoise light, fixed along the coast.

Accessories: A white aero helmet with a clear visor (worn from Frame 2), grip gloves, an impact vest, the thin titanium wrist cuff, the kill-cord lanyard.

Hair: The same tight low braid, tucked into the collar, tail flicking in the spray.

Makeup: Matte sun-warmed complexion, defined brow, cool eye, neutral lip; salt-spray sheen permitted.

Movement (the one impossible move): As the jet-ski carves hard and throws a great fan of spray, the water does not fall — it suspends into ribbons of liquid glass that hang shimmering and refracting the gold light, then drip away as light-droplets.

Concept Line: "She carves the coast — and the sea hangs in the air to watch."

Duration: ~24 seconds · 8 frames · 24fps · 180° shutter.

Mood: Bright, salt-spray, exhilarant, sublime.

Composition: Tall cliff verticals, swell leading-lines, spray foregrounds, the ski raked across thirds; intercut a low chase plate, a wave-ramp jump angle, a sea-cave near-miss, and a hero stop. Rich layered foreground-to-background depth, elegant spatial hierarchy and clean visual flow (original framing and angle preserved).

Grade: Turquoise-and-gold, deep sea-blue shadow, warm cliff-ochre, liquid-glass highlights for the magic, rich blacks, controlled bloom on spray and sun. Premium editorial grade — rich clean colour, luminous mid-tones, deep-but-clean blacks, controlled elegant saturation, no faded or muddy tones, natural true skin preserved.

Identity Safety: Aira rendered entirely from the locked reference sheet; adult, 25; no physical or ethnic enumeration; full-coverage wardrobe; stylized stunt, damage on the ski and environment only.

### 🎚️ Master Soundtrack & Sound-Design Brief — Audio Block B (outside the prompts)

Role: The continuous score and sound-design bed under the in-frame diegetic audio across all eight frames, binding them into one twenty-four-second piece. Genre & palette: Bright tropical-cinematic electronic — plucked synth, organic hand-percussion, a warm bass, a soaring glassy lead reserved for the liquid-glass magic, and sea-and-gull ambience. Tempo & key: 122 BPM, A major (turning to A minor for tension), 4/4, locked so jumps and impacts land on the downbeat. Instrumentation by layer: sub and warm bass (ski power), hand-percussion and kick (forward drive), bright pluck-arp (momentum), shimmering glass lead and bells (the liquid-glass magic), granular sea-spray-and-gull texture (atmosphere). Dynamic arc: Frames 1–2 bright and open (pluck and sea only); Frames 3–5 full percussion and bass enter with the run and jump; Frame 6 the glass lead blooms over a riser; Frame 7 a half-time breakdown for the sea-cave near-miss; Frame 8 full triumphant tutti tailing to the opening pluck for a perfect loop. Hits & transitions: sub-drop on the launch (F2), a percussion riser into the wave-ramp jump (F5), a beat-dropout and 250ms suspended silence at the jump apex (F5→F6), an impact boom and brief near-silence at the sea-cave near-miss (F7), a glass-and-percussion stinger on the hero stop (F8). Mix: score about 6 dB under engine and spray-impacts; sidechain the bed to the biggest throttle-bursts; wide-stereo glass and pads, mono-tight sub; tape warmth, sea-air reverb on the glass lead. Loop point: the closing pluck matches bar one.

MASTER AUDIO: a 48-second custom cinematic score (8 clips × 6s) composed in self-resolving ~6-second phrases, so it can be trimmed to the reel’s assembled length at any phrase boundary without a hard cut — it always resolves, never stops dead or loops audibly. Built on deep trailer percussion, a driving synth bass and brass swells — premium, cinematic and emotionally catching, never generic stock or empty ambience. It opens immediately on a low brass-and-sub impact (no fade-in) so the mood lands within the first second. Emotional arc: an airy intimate open across the first beats, a rising shimmer of anticipation toward frame 5, the warm emotional peak on the signature move — As the jet-ski carves hard and throws a great fan of spray, the water does not…, then a graceful resolve back toward the opening for an invisible loop. SPOKEN reel — the score opens space for the spoken line at its beat and ducks gently beneath the voice, then re-blooms after. Layered as one system — lead melody, warm sub for body, taut percussive tension as atmosphere, restrained percussion, and event impacts reserved for the major beats, each tuned to the score’s own key so every hit reads as the music itself expanding, never an added sound effect.

### Frame 1 of 8 — IMAGE PROMPT
STRICT COLOUR & RENDER MANDATE (must be followed exactly; this governs render QUALITY only and must NOT change the composition, camera, lens, framing, wardrobe, action or identity specified above): render the colours already specified at their richest, freshest, cleanest and most premium — crisp, vivid and luminous with elegant, controlled saturation; never dull, faded, washed-out, muddy, grey or flat. IDENTITY LOCK: Aira [paste identity-lock reference sheet here]. Scene: she sits astride the idling jet-ski in a turquoise cove beneath towering golden cliffs, helmet under one arm, reading the open coastline and the rolling swell ahead. WARDROBE: coral-and-white race suit with reflective piping, collar up, beaded with sea-spray. FOOTWEAR: white neoprene race boots with coral soles, one in the foot-well, one trailing near the water. EYE COLOUR: warm amber-brown, gold-on-turquoise catchlight, gaze along the coast. ACCESSORIES: grip gloves, impact vest, aero helmet at the hip, titanium cuff, kill-cord clipped to the vest. HAIR: tight low braid tucked to the collar, tail damp. MAKEUP: matte sun-warmed skin, defined brow, cool eye, neutral lip. HANDS & NAILS: short clean nails, gloved hand resting on the bars. IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

FRAMING: ASPECT 9:16, heroic low angle, Aira centred and rising, ski anchored beneath. SUBJECT FRAMING & POSITION: Framed as heroic low angle, Aira centred and rising, ski anchored beneath, on a low hero angle looking up. The figure is framed for motion. Her body is alive and committed to the motion, in dynamically angled to camera. Camera distance follows the shot size, holding the action sharp. The beat: the hero stop — the jet-ski carves to a triumphant spray-stop in a sunlit turquoise bay, Aira risen to stand on the foot-wells, helmet off and held at her hip, the last liquid-glass ribbons dripping into light around her against towering gold cliffs and open sea. Her pose reads as tall and open, weight grounded, shoulders dropping back, a victor's stillness — gaze sweeping the swells with steady nerve; head squared into the gale; both shoulders square to the threat and braced; the RIGHT hand gripping the tiller or rail with knuckles set while the LEFT arm reaches out for balance; torso braced wide and low against the pitch, core centred; waist low, hips squared over the deck; the RIGHT leg and LEFT leg braced wide on the pitching wet deck with knees soft; both grip-soled feet planted and weighted low, riding the heave; soaked braid whipping in the gale. ORIENTATION LOCK — preserve this exact left-to-right composition; do not mirror, flip or invert the frame. LENS + DOF + BOKEH: 35mm-equivalent, f/4, droplets in soft bokeh, sharp on her. ATMOSPHERE: dripping light-droplets, settling spray, sea serenity. COLOR GRADE: rich turquoise-and-gold, deep sea-blue shadow, gentle bloom, clean blacks. TEXTURE & MICRO-DETAIL: skin and salt-sheen, spray on the suit, fading glass-filaments, the ski's accumulated battle scars (salt-scuffed hull, chipped nose, scraped sponson) all visible. NEGATIVE: no extra people, no warped face, no logos, no oversaturation, no sexualization, no enumeration, no text.

### Frame 8 of 8 — VIDEO PROMPT
STRICT COLOUR & RENDER MANDATE (must be followed exactly; this governs render QUALITY only and must NOT change the composition, camera, lens, framing, wardrobe, action or identity specified above): render the colours already specified at their richest, freshest, cleanest and most premium — crisp, vivid and luminous with elegant, controlled saturation; never dull, faded, washed-out, muddy, grey or flat. Hold deep-but-clean blacks, luminous full mid-tones and beautiful highlight roll-off with high dynamic range and no blown highlights. Wherever sky is visible, render it richly graded and cinematic — never empty, white or overexposed. Wherever water is visible, render it crystal-clear with deep sea-blue and turquoise gradients, clean caustics and sparkling reflections — never grey or murky (unless the concept explicitly specifies dark / ink / obsidian water, which stays deep, glassy and clean). Protect natural, true skin tone; never push saturation onto the face. Keep the image immaculately clean and premium with clear figure-to-ground separation so the hero reads first; honour the concept's intended dominant surface and full-frame coverage, and do NOT reveal unwanted structural edges, seams, side-walls or empty dead space that break the intended composition. Photoreal, award-winning editorial finish — no AI artifacts, plastic or waxy skin, dead eyes, malformed hands or unfinished backgrounds.

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: Heroic low-angle finale shot. CAMERA MOVEMENT: one move only — a slow push-in and slight rise settling on Aira standing. LENS: 35mm-equivalent, f/4. PHYSICS: the ski carves and the hull settles off-plane into the bay with a final bob; Aira rises against residual momentum; the last glass-ribbons lose cohesion and drip into light-droplets that fall and fade; spray settles. SUBJECT + OBJECT ACTION (timecoded): 0.0–2.0s the ski carves to a spray-stop in the bay, settling off-plane with a bob (spray drifting forward); 2.0–4.0s Aira pushes up to stand on the foot-wells and lifts the helmet off, braid loosening; 4.0–6.0s she exhales and a brief fierce smile breaks as the last glass-droplets drip and wink out around her. EXPRESSION TRANSITION: into into — hard focus melts to a heaving-chest release at 2.8s, resolving into eased-brow triumph and a brief fierce smile by 5.2s. LIGHTING: warm sun key, bright rim, glass-droplet practicals. ENVIRONMENT: sunlit turquoise bay, gold cliffs, open sea. AUDIO (Block A): the engine spooling down to an idle then off, lapping water, gulls, a long exhale at 2.8s, a final crystalline shimmer as the droplets fade; score lands a glass-and-percussion stinger then tails to the opening pluck for the loop. DURATION: 6.0s. FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180°, gentle blur on the rise, crisp on the face. LIP-SYNC: none; an exhale and a closed-lip smile. NEGATIVE: no cut, no second move, no extra people, no warped geometry, no text or watermark.

---

## Concept 09 — Speedboat Deck Fight

Lane: Water + unarmed combat · a boarding fight across the decks of two racing speedboats.

Format: Seven-frame vertical action reel — one continuous deck-to-deck unarmed fight between two offshore speedboats running side by side on the open sea at high noon, rendered as seven seeded keyframes, roughly twenty-one seconds.

Hero Vehicle: A pearl-white offshore race boat with coral and teal accents, twin outboards, a low deck and grab-rails; progressive realistic damage — salt-scuff, a cracked windscreen corner, a scraped rail — never swamped, always running.

Rival Element: A matte-charcoal offshore boat running rail-to-rail; a single unarmed pursuer in dark deck-gear that Aira fights hand-to-hand — no weapons, contact landing on guard, pads, and gear only.

Setting: The open sea at high noon, deep sapphire water under a hard bright sky, long swell, the two boats locked side by side at speed, wakes braiding, spray sheeting between the hulls. Every zone of the frame intentionally designed, immaculately clean and premium, with layered foreground, midground and background depth and rich environmental storytelling. Water rendered crystal-clear in rich sea-blue and turquoise gradients, with beautiful caustics and clean sparkling reflections. Sky richly graded and cinematic with elegant clouds and premium light, never flat or empty.

Wardrobe: A fitted teal-and-white water-combat suit with reflective piping, articulated armour panels at shoulder and shin, high collar — full-coverage, modest, watersport-coded.

Footwear: White deck-grip boots with coral soles, ankle-locked, siped for wet decks — important, since the fight is foot-driven.

Eye Colour: Warm amber-brown (consistent locked identity), hard in the noon glare, fixed on the pursuer.

Accessories: Grip gloves, a slim impact vest, the thin titanium wrist cuff, a clipped safety lanyard (released before the leap); no helmet (deck fight).

Hair: The same tight low braid, salt-damp, tail whipping in the wind.

Makeup: Matte sun-flushed complexion, defined brow, cool eye, neutral lip; sweat and salt-spray sheen permitted.

Movement (the one impossible move): At the apex of her signature spinning kick, the spray sheeting between the boats does not fall — it rises into a towering curved wall of water that hangs like glass behind her, mirroring the strike, then collapses back into the sea.

Concept Line: "She fights across two boats at speed — and the sea stands up behind her."

Duration: ~21 seconds · 7 frames · 24fps · 180° shutter.

Mood: Hard, bright, kinetic, sublime.

Composition: Low rail-level horizons, wake leading-lines, deck foregrounds, the two figures raked across thirds; intercut a rail-to-rail two-shot, a boarding leap, a 360-kick hero beat, a water-wall reveal, and a hero stop. Rich layered foreground-to-background depth, elegant spatial hierarchy and clean visual flow (original framing and angle preserved).

Grade: Sapphire-and-white, hard noon contrast, teal accents, glassy blue-white for the water-wall magic, rich shadows, controlled bloom on spray and sun. World-class editorial colour — rich and vivid yet clean, luminous mid-tones, deep-but-clean blacks, controlled saturation, nothing washed-out, natural skin protected.

Identity Safety: Aira rendered entirely from the locked reference sheet; adult, 25; no physical or ethnic enumeration; full-coverage wardrobe; stylized unarmed combat — impacts land on guard, pads, and gear only, no gore, no weapons.

### 🎚️ Master Soundtrack & Sound-Design Brief — Audio Block B (outside the prompts)

Role: The continuous score and sound-design bed under the in-frame diegetic audio across all seven frames, binding them into one twenty-one-second piece. Genre & palette: Hard hybrid-cinematic — driving taiko-and-tom percussion, a sub-heavy bass, a tense brass-and-string ostinato, a soaring glassy choir reserved for the water-wall magic, and sea-and-engine ambience. Tempo & key: 124 BPM, D minor, 4/4, locked so strikes and boarding land on the downbeat. Instrumentation by layer: sub and distorted bass (engines and mass), taiko and toms (the strikes), tense brass-and-string ostinato (the fight), glassy choir and bells (the water-wall magic), granular sea-spray-and-wind texture (atmosphere). Dynamic arc: Frame 1 sparse and tense (drone and sea only); Frames 2–3 percussion and bass enter with the leap and clash; Frame 4 a brass stab on the pursuer's attack; Frame 5 a taiko riser into the 360 kick with a single huge hit on contact; Frame 6 the glassy choir blooms over a riser as the water-wall rises; Frame 7 a triumphant final hit tailing to the opening drone for a perfect loop. Hits & transitions: sub-drop on the boarding leap (F2), a brass stab on the pursuer's strike (F4), a taiko riser into the spin (F5), one massive impact-hit and a 250ms beat-dropout on the kick's contact (F5), a choir-swell as the water-wall rises (F6), a brass-and-bell stinger on the hero stop (F7). Mix: score about 6 dB under engines and combat-impacts; sidechain the bed to the biggest hits; wide-stereo brass and choir, mono-tight sub; tape saturation, vast sea-reverb on the choir. Loop point: the closing drone matches bar one.

MASTER AUDIO: a 42-second custom cinematic score (7 clips × 6s) composed in self-resolving ~6-second phrases, so it can be trimmed to the reel’s assembled length at any phrase boundary without a hard cut — it always resolves, never stops dead or loops audibly. Built on deep trailer percussion, a driving synth bass and brass swells — premium, cinematic and emotionally catching, never generic stock or empty ambience. It opens immediately on a low brass-and-sub impact (no fade-in) so the mood lands within the first second. Emotional arc: an airy intimate open across the first beats, a rising shimmer of anticipation toward frame 5, the warm emotional peak on the signature move — At the apex of her signature spinning kick, the spray sheeting between the boats does not…, then a graceful resolve back toward the opening for an invisible loop. SPOKEN reel — the score opens space for the spoken line at its beat and ducks gently beneath the voice, then re-blooms after. Layered as one system — lead melody, warm sub for body, taut percussive tension as atmosphere, restrained percussion, and event impacts reserved for the major beats, each tuned to the score’s own key so every hit reads as the music itself expanding, never an added sound effect.

The beat: the magic blooms — the spray sheeting between the boats rises behind Aira into a towering curved wall of water, hanging like glass and refracting the noon sun, mirroring her finished strike as the pursuer reels at the rail. Her pose reads as weight settling from the kick, chest lifting, shoulders opening with exhilaration — gaze drilling into the strike's mark, eyes fierce; head squared and driving behind the blow; the RIGHT shoulder whipping forward through the strike while the LEFT shoulder tucks in guard; the RIGHT arm driving the blow with the fist or edge leading and the forearm corded while the LEFT hand stays raised in a tight guard; torso torqued through the hips into the strike, core rotated; waist whipping with the rotation, hips driving forward; the RIGHT rear leg pivoting on the ball as it drives while the LEFT front leg braces; both feet driving the ground, weight transferring forward through the blow; hair whipping with the torque. ORIENTATION LOCK — preserve this exact left-to-right composition; do not mirror, flip or invert the frame. LENS + DOF + BOKEH: 85mm-equivalent, f/2.8, water-wall in luminous bokeh, sharp on her face. ATMOSPHERE: a suspended curved wall of water, drifting light-droplets. COLOR GRADE: glassy blue-white water-wall, sapphire shadow for contrast, gentle bloom. TEXTURE & MICRO-DETAIL: skin, fine refraction and internal swirl in the water-wall, caustic glints, spray on the suit catching light. NEGATIVE: no extra people beyond the pursuer, no weapons, no warped face, no garish clipping, no logos, no oversaturation beyond intended bloom, no sexualization, no enumeration, no text.

### Frame 6 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT
STRICT COLOUR & RENDER MANDATE (must be followed exactly; this governs render QUALITY only and must NOT change the composition, camera, lens, framing, wardrobe, action or identity specified above): render the colours already specified at their richest, freshest, cleanest and most premium — crisp, vivid and luminous with elegant, controlled saturation; never dull, faded, washed-out, muddy, grey or flat. Hold deep-but-clean blacks, luminous full mid-tones and beautiful highlight roll-off with high dynamic range and no blown highlights. Wherever sky is visible, render it richly graded and cinematic — never empty, white or overexposed. Wherever water is visible, render it crystal-clear with deep sea-blue and turquoise gradients, clean caustics and sparkling reflections — never grey or murky (unless the concept explicitly specifies dark / ink / obsidian water, which stays deep, glassy and clean). Protect natural, true skin tone; never push saturation onto the face. Keep the image immaculately clean and premium with clear figure-to-ground separation so the hero reads first; honour the concept's intended dominant surface and full-frame coverage, and do NOT reveal unwanted structural edges, seams, side-walls or empty dead space that break the intended composition. Photoreal, award-winning editorial finish — no AI artifacts, plastic or waxy skin, dead eyes, malformed hands or unfinished backgrounds.

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: Hero three-quarter reveal shot. CAMERA MOVEMENT: one move only — a slow rise-and-settle (crane up) revealing the towering water-wall. LENS: 85mm-equivalent, f/2.8. PHYSICS: the spray between the hulls defies gravity, rising and halting into a towering curved wall of water that holds its arc, refracts the sun into caustics, churns internally, then loses cohesion and collapses back into the sea; light bends through the mass. SUBJECT + OBJECT ACTION (timecoded): 0.0–2.0s as Aira recovers from the kick, the sheeting spray slows and begins to climb behind her (droplets rising unnaturally); 2.0–4.0s it knits into a towering curved water-wall hanging like glass, mirroring her, the pursuer reeling at the rail; 4.0–6.0s the wall churns and begins to collapse back into the sea, droplets raining slow. EXPRESSION TRANSITION: into into — strained effort releases into parted-lip awe at 2.4s as the wall rises, blooming into a wild grin by 4.8s. LIGHTING: sun refracted through the water-wall casting caustics on her face, glowing rim. ENVIRONMENT: the sea standing up in a curved wall between the boats. AUDIO (Block A): engines easing under, a deep rising water-rush and a shimmering crystalline swell as the wall climbs at 2.0s, a soft delighted exhale-laugh at 4.8s; the glassy choir blooms over a riser. DURATION: 6.0s. FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180°, silky blur on the rise, crisp water-wall. LIP-SYNC: none; a breath of wonder. NEGATIVE: no cut, no second move, no cartoonish glow, no weapons, no warped boat, no text.

## Concept 10 — Waterfall Kayak Plunge Duel

Lane: Water vehicle · a whitewater kayak duel down a jungle gorge to a great waterfall plunge.

Format: Eight-frame vertical action reel — one continuous whitewater kayak run and waterfall plunge down a misted jungle gorge at morning, rendered as eight seeded keyframes, roughly twenty-four seconds.

Hero Vehicle: A pearl-white creek kayak with teal and gold accents, sharp rocker, a snug cockpit and a carbon paddle; progressive realistic damage — scuffed hull, a chipped bow, a scraped blade — never holed, always driving.

Rival Element: A matte-charcoal kayak running the same lines, trading position through the rapids; takes its own scuffs and never breaks apart.

Setting: A misted emerald jungle gorge at morning, tea-dark whitewater roaring between mossy basalt walls, hanging vines and shafts of god-ray light, building toward a thundering tiered waterfall and a green plunge-pool far below. Every zone of the frame intentionally designed, immaculately clean and premium, with layered foreground, midground and background depth and rich environmental storytelling. Water rendered crystal-clear in rich sea-blue and turquoise gradients, with beautiful caustics and clean sparkling reflections. Greenery lush and healthy with premium landscaping.

Wardrobe: A fitted teal-and-white whitewater drysuit with reflective piping, articulated panels, high collar and a snug spray-skirt — full-coverage, modest, watersport-coded.

Footwear: White neoprene paddle-boots with coral soles, ankle-snug, grippy for the cockpit braces.

Eye Colour: Warm amber-brown (consistent locked identity), catching the green-gold gorge light, fixed on the line.

Accessories: A white whitewater helmet with a clear visor (worn throughout), a buoyancy vest, grip gloves, the thin titanium wrist cuff, a carbon paddle.

Hair: The same tight low braid, tucked into the collar, tail flicking through the spray.

Makeup: Matte fresh complexion, defined brow, cool eye, neutral lip; river-spray sheen permitted.

Movement (the one impossible move): At the lip of the great waterfall, the falling water does not simply plunge — it crystallizes into a descending staircase of glowing crystal steps that the kayak skips down like stairs, each step shattering to light behind her, before the water resumes its fall.

Concept Line: "She runs the falls — and the water builds her a staircase of light."

Duration: ~24 seconds · 8 frames · 24fps · 180° shutter.

Mood: Lush, thunderous, exhilarant, sublime.

Composition: Tall gorge verticals, current leading-lines, whitewater foregrounds, the kayaks raked across thirds; intercut a low chase plate, a drop boof-stroke, a waterfall-lip reveal, a plunge-pool near-miss, and a hero eddy-out. Premium foreground, midground and background layering with strong three-dimensional depth and clean visual balance (original framing preserved).

Grade: Emerald-and-gold, tea-dark water, mossy shadow, glowing crystal-cyan for the staircase magic, rich blacks, controlled bloom on mist and god-rays. Premium editorial grade — rich clean colour, luminous mid-tones, deep-but-clean blacks, controlled elegant saturation, no faded or muddy tones, natural true skin preserved.

Identity Safety: Aira rendered entirely from the locked reference sheet; adult, 25; no physical or ethnic enumeration; full-coverage wardrobe; stylized stunt, scuffs on the kayaks and environment only.

### 🎚️ Master Soundtrack & Sound-Design Brief — Audio Block B (outside the prompts)

Role: The continuous score and sound-design bed under the in-frame diegetic audio across all eight frames, binding them into one twenty-four-second piece. Genre & palette: Lush adventure-cinematic — organic log-drums and tribal percussion, a warm bass, a soaring string-and-flute theme, a shimmering crystal-bell motif reserved for the staircase magic, and jungle-and-water ambience. Tempo & key: 118 BPM, E minor, 4/4, locked so drops and strokes land on the downbeat. Instrumentation by layer: sub and warm bass (the river's power), log-drums and toms (the strokes and drops), strings-and-flute (the run), shimmering crystal-bells and choir (the staircase magic), granular water-roar-and-bird texture (atmosphere). Dynamic arc: Frames 1–2 lush and building (flute and water only); Frames 3–5 full percussion and bass enter with the run and drops; Frame 6 the crystal-bell theme blooms over a riser at the waterfall-lip; Frame 7 a half-time breakdown for the plunge-pool near-miss; Frame 8 full triumphant tutti tailing to the opening flute for a perfect loop. Hits & transitions: sub-drop on the launch (F2), a log-drum riser into the boof-drop (F5), a beat-dropout and 250ms suspended silence at the waterfall-lip (F5→F6), an impact boom and brief near-silence at the plunge-pool landing (F7), a crystal-and-string stinger on the hero eddy-out (F8). Mix: score about 6 dB under the water-roar and stroke-impacts; sidechain the bed to the biggest drops; wide-stereo strings and bells, mono-tight sub; tape warmth, gorge-reverb on the bells. Loop point: the closing flute matches bar one.

MASTER AUDIO: a 48-second custom cinematic score (8 clips × 6s) composed in self-resolving ~6-second phrases, so it can be trimmed to the reel’s assembled length at any phrase boundary without a hard cut — it always resolves, never stops dead or loops audibly. Built on a delicate felt piano, atmospheric pads and elegant glass textures — premium, cinematic and emotionally catching, never generic stock or empty ambience. It opens immediately on a soft piano strike with a low sub-bloom (no fade-in) so the mood lands within the first second. Emotional arc: an airy intimate open across the first beats, a rising shimmer of anticipation toward frame 5, the warm emotional peak on the signature move — At the lip of the great waterfall, the falling water does not simply plunge, then a graceful resolve back toward the opening for an invisible loop. SPOKEN reel — the score opens space for the spoken line at its beat and ducks gently beneath the voice, then re-blooms after. Layered as one system — lead melody, warm sub for body, elegant glassy shimmer as atmosphere, restrained percussion, and event impacts reserved for the major beats, each tuned to the score’s own key so every hit reads as the music itself expanding, never an added sound effect.

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SUBJECT + OBJECT ACTION (timecoded): 0.0–1.6s Aira drives a huge boof-stroke and the kayak launches off the pourover, nose lifting over the hole (sieve passing below); 1.6–3.6s boat and paddler hang at the apex, level, spray-beads suspended; 3.6–6.0s the arc tips toward descent, the waterfall-lip sliding into view beyond. EXPRESSION TRANSITION: into — wide-eyed shock clamps into bared-teeth, neck-corded effort by 1.6s, held through the apex. LIGHTING: god-ray rim, emerald uplight, suspended sparkle on the beads. ENVIRONMENT: above the hole and sieve, gorge walls. AUDIO (Block A): a paddle-pry grunt and boof-splash at 0.4s, wind-rush peaking then thinning at the apex, a guttural effort-grunt at 2.0s; score log-drum riser then a held breath. DURATION: 6.0s. FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180°, smooth arc blur, crisp paddler. LIP-SYNC: none; a strained effort sound. NEGATIVE: no entrapment, no cut, no warped kayak, no text, no gore.

## Concept 11 — Jade Bamboo Sword Duel

Lane: Weapon · a wuxia-style sword duel through a glowing jade bamboo forest.

Format: Seven-frame vertical action reel — one continuous bladed duel through a misted jade bamboo grove at dawn, rendered as seven seeded keyframes, roughly twenty-one seconds.

Hero Weapon: A slender straight jian (double-edged sword) with a jade-green silk tassel and a pale wood scabbard; the polished blade catches dawn-light and trails a faint jade-qi shimmer; realistic wear only — a few honest nicks — never bloodied, contact landing on the rival's blade, guard, and bamboo only.

Rival Element: A masked duelist with a matching jian, trading clean wuxia exchanges; takes only blade-scuffs and is disarmed, never wounded.

Setting: A vast grove of luminous jade-green bamboo at dawn, shafts of gold light slicing low mist, a moss-stone path and a still black pond, leaves drifting, the canopy hissing in a soft wind. Every zone of the frame intentionally designed, immaculately clean and premium, with layered foreground, midground and background depth and rich environmental storytelling. Sky richly graded and cinematic with elegant clouds and premium light, never flat or empty. Greenery lush and healthy with premium landscaping.

Wardrobe: A fitted teal-and-jade martial robe with wrapped sash, articulated sleeves bound at the forearm, high collar and side-slit trousers over leg-wraps — full-coverage, modest, wuxia-coded.

Footwear: Black cloth martial boots with jade laces and thin grippy soles, silent on the moss-stone.

Eye Colour: Warm amber-brown (consistent locked identity), catching jade-gold light, fixed on the rival blade.

Accessories: Forearm wraps, a jade ring on the thumb, the thin titanium wrist cuff, the silk-tasseled jian and pale scabbard at the sash.

Hair: The same tight low braid, bound with a jade cord, tail flicking with each turn.

Makeup: Matte fresh complexion, defined brow, cool eye, neutral lip; dawn-dew sheen permitted.

Movement (the one impossible move): At the height of a spinning blade-flourish, every falling bamboo leaf in the grove freezes in mid-air and turns slowly, hanging like jade confetti, while her blade draws a glowing ribbon of jade-qi through the suspended leaves — then time resumes and the leaves fall.

Concept Line: "She duels through the bamboo — and the falling leaves hold their breath."

Duration: ~21 seconds · 7 frames · 24fps · 180° shutter.

Mood: Serene, precise, breathtaking, sublime.

Composition: Tall bamboo verticals, path leading-lines, leaf-drift foregrounds, the two duelists raked across thirds; intercut a guard-up two-shot, a blade-clash, a spinning-disarm hero beat, a leaf-freeze reveal, and a hero stillness. Premium foreground, midground and background layering with strong three-dimensional depth and clean visual balance (original framing preserved).

Grade: Jade-and-gold, deep forest shadow, misty teal, glowing jade-cyan for the qi magic, rich blacks, controlled bloom on mist and god-rays. World-class editorial colour — rich and vivid yet clean, luminous mid-tones, deep-but-clean blacks, controlled saturation, nothing washed-out, natural skin protected.

Identity Safety: Aira rendered entirely from the locked reference sheet; adult, 25; no physical or ethnic enumeration; full-coverage wardrobe; stylized bladework — contact lands on blade, guard, and bamboo only, no blood, no wounds.

### 🎚️ Master Soundtrack & Sound-Design Brief — Audio Block B (outside the prompts)

Role: The continuous score and sound-design bed under the in-frame diegetic audio across all seven frames, binding them into one twenty-one-second piece. Genre & palette: Wuxia-cinematic — erhu and guzheng, a breathy dizi flute, a soft taiko pulse, a shimmering bell-and-choir theme reserved for the leaf-freeze magic, and bamboo-and-wind ambience. Tempo & key: 96 BPM, D Dorian, 4/4, rubato-friendly so clashes land on the accent. Instrumentation by layer: low taiko and frame-drum (footwork and lunges), guzheng runs (the exchanges), erhu and dizi (the lyrical line), shimmering bells and wordless choir (the leaf-freeze magic), granular bamboo-creak-and-wind texture (atmosphere). Dynamic arc: Frame 1 spare and breathing (dizi and wind only); Frames 2–3 taiko and guzheng enter with the engage and clash; Frame 4 an erhu stab on the rival's attack; Frame 5 a guzheng riser into the spinning disarm; Frame 6 the bell-and-choir theme blooms in near-silence as the leaves freeze; Frame 7 a resolving erhu phrase tailing to the opening dizi for a perfect loop. Hits & transitions: a taiko hit on the first lunge (F2), ringing blade-clang accents (F3–F4), a guzheng glissando riser into the disarm (F5), a full beat-dropout and 300ms suspended silence as the leaves freeze (F5→F6), a single bell-toll and choir-swell through the frozen grove (F6), a soft erhu-and-bell resolve on the hero stillness (F7). Mix: score about 6 dB under the blade-and-foot foley; sidechain the bed to the biggest clangs; wide-stereo erhu and choir, mono-tight taiko; tape warmth, vast grove-reverb on the bells. Loop point: the closing dizi matches bar one.

MASTER AUDIO: a 42-second custom cinematic score (7 clips × 6s) composed in self-resolving ~6-second phrases, so it can be trimmed to the reel’s assembled length at any phrase boundary without a hard cut — it always resolves, never stops dead or loops audibly. Built on a delicate felt piano, atmospheric pads and elegant glass textures — premium, cinematic and emotionally catching, never generic stock or empty ambience. It opens immediately on a soft piano strike with a low sub-bloom (no fade-in) so the mood lands within the first second. Emotional arc: an airy intimate open across the first beats, a rising shimmer of anticipation toward frame 5, the warm emotional peak on the signature move — At the height of a spinning blade-flourish, every falling bamboo leaf in the grove freezes in…, then a graceful resolve back toward the opening for an invisible loop. SILENT reel — no spoken line; an optional wordless breath/choir texture may rise on the peak only if it elevates, never a lyric. Layered as one system — lead melody, warm sub for body, elegant glassy shimmer as atmosphere, restrained percussion, and event impacts reserved for the major beats, each tuned to the score’s own key so every hit reads as the music itself expanding, never an added sound effect.

### Frame 1 of 7 — IMAGE PROMPT
STRICT COLOUR & RENDER MANDATE (must be followed exactly; this governs render QUALITY only and must NOT change the composition, camera, lens, framing, wardrobe, action or identity specified above): render the colours already specified at their richest, freshest, cleanest and most premium — crisp, vivid and luminous with elegant, controlled saturation; never dull, faded, washed-out, muddy, grey or flat. Hold deep-but-clean blacks, luminous full mid-tones and beautiful highlight roll-off with high dynamic range and no blown highlights. Wherever sky is visible, render it richly graded and cinematic — never empty, white or overexposed. Wherever water is visible, render it crystal-clear with deep sea-blue and turquoise gradients, clean caustics and sparkling reflections — never grey or murky (unless the concept explicitly specifies dark / ink / obsidian water, which stays deep, glassy and clean). Protect natural, true skin tone; never push saturation onto the face. Keep the image immaculately clean and premium with clear figure-to-ground separation so the hero reads first; honour the concept's intended dominant surface and full-frame coverage, and do NOT reveal unwanted structural edges, seams, side-walls or empty dead space that break the intended composition. Photoreal, award-winning editorial finish — no AI artifacts, plastic or waxy skin, dead eyes, malformed hands or unfinished backgrounds.

IDENTITY LOCK: Aira [paste identity-lock reference sheet here]. Scene: she stands on the moss-stone path in the jade bamboo grove at dawn, jian held low and ready in a relaxed guard, reading the masked duelist standing still across the drifting leaves. WARDROBE: teal-and-jade martial robe, sash wrapped, sleeves bound, collar up, dew-beaded. FOOTWEAR: black cloth martial boots set lightly on the moss-stone. EYE COLOUR: warm amber-brown, jade-gold catchlight, gaze on the rival. ACCESSORIES: forearm wraps, jade thumb-ring, titanium cuff, scabbard at the sash, silk tassel stirring. HAIR: tight low braid bound with jade cord, tail still. MAKEUP: matte fresh skin, defined brow, cool eye, neutral lip. HANDS & NAILS: short clean nails, sure two-finger-and-thumb grip on the hilt. EXPRESSION: cold-focus game face — steady level gaze across the grove, lips in a strong neutral set, a slow controlled breath, calm intensity, faint jaw-tension. BODY POSTURE & WEIGHT: weight sunk and centred in a low ready stance, blade angled across the body, shoulders settled, living stillness — breath, tassel, and drifting leaves alive. SPATIAL LOGIC: foreground a drifting leaf and the blade-tip; midground Aira in guard; background the masked rival, bamboo columns, mist, a still black pond. LIGHTING: KEY low gold dawn god-rays from camera-left; FILL jade canopy-bounce; RIM bright edge on braid and blade; SEPARATION grove-mist. FRAMING: ASPECT 9:16, medium-wide, low camera, path leading in, bamboo towering. SUBJECT FRAMING & POSITION: Framed as medium-wide, low camera, path leading in, bamboo towering, on a low hero angle looking up. Most of the figure is in frame with the environment around her. Her body is alive and committed to the motion, in dynamically angled to camera. Camera distance follows the shot size, holding the action sharp. The beat: she stands on the moss-stone path in the jade bamboo grove at dawn, jian held low and ready in a relaxed guard, reading the masked duelist standing still across the drifting leaves. Her pose reads as weight sunk and centred in a low ready stance, blade angled across the body, shoulders settled, living stillness — breath, tassel, and drifting leaves alive — gaze fierce and narrowed through the slide, reading the exit; head braced and steady against the lateral G; both shoulders driving the counter-steer; the RIGHT arm cranking full opposite-lock with forearm cords standing out while the LEFT arm braces the wheel; torso thrown against the lateral G, braced and coiled; waist torqued, hips wedged into the seat; the RIGHT leg feeding throttle mid-slide while the LEFT boot stays hard on the floor; both feet committed, weight slung outward against the slide; braid flung across the shoulder by the lateral throw. ORIENTATION LOCK — preserve this exact left-to-right composition; do not mirror, flip or invert the frame. LENS + DOF + BOKEH: 35mm-equivalent, f/2.8, sharp on her and the blade, rival to bokeh. ATMOSPHERE: drifting leaves, low mist, god-ray shafts. COLOR GRADE: jade-and-gold, deep forest shadow, misty teal, controlled bloom. TEXTURE & MICRO-DETAIL: pores and dew, woven robe fabric, polished blade with faint nicks, bamboo-node and moss detail. NEGATIVE: no extra people beyond the rival, no facial blur, no distorted hands, no blood, no logos or text, no warped blade geometry, no oversaturation, no sexualization, no physical or ethnic enumeration, no watermark.

### Frame 1 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT
STRICT COLOUR & RENDER MANDATE (must be followed exactly; this governs render QUALITY only and must NOT change the composition, camera, lens, framing, wardrobe, action or identity specified above): render the colours already specified at their richest, freshest, cleanest and most premium — crisp, vivid and luminous with elegant, controlled saturation; never dull, faded, washed-out, muddy, grey or flat. Hold deep-but-clean blacks, luminous full mid-tones and beautiful highlight roll-off with high dynamic range and no blown highlights. Wherever sky is visible, render it richly graded and cinematic — never empty, white or overexposed. Wherever water is visible, render it crystal-clear with deep sea-blue and turquoise gradients, clean caustics and sparkling reflections — never grey or murky (unless the concept explicitly specifies dark / ink / obsidian water, which stays deep, glassy and clean). Protect natural, true skin tone; never push saturation onto the face. Keep the image immaculately clean and premium with clear figure-to-ground separation so the hero reads first; honour the concept's intended dominant surface and full-frame coverage, and do NOT reveal unwanted structural edges, seams, side-walls or empty dead space that break the intended composition. Photoreal, award-winning editorial finish — no AI artifacts, plastic or waxy skin, dead eyes, malformed hands or unfinished backgrounds.

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: Medium-wide hero establishing shot, low angle. CAMERA MOVEMENT: one move only — a slow 1.2x push-in toward Aira as she reads the rival. LENS: 35mm-equivalent, f/2.8. PHYSICS: leaves drift and settle with realistic air-float; the silk tassel sways; mist curls through god-rays; the blade holds steady with a faint jade shimmer; no contact yet. SUBJECT + OBJECT ACTION (timecoded): 0.0–2.0s Aira exhales a slow breath and studies the rival, fingers settling on the hilt (leaves drifting, tassel swaying); 2.0–4.0s she sinks a fraction deeper into stance, blade tip rising a degree, gaze hardening; 4.0–6.0s she holds, the only motion drifting leaves and the stirring tassel. EXPRESSION TRANSITION: throughout — neutral set tightening to a faint eye-narrow by 5.0s as resolve gathers; no smile. LIGHTING: gold dawn key, jade bounce, grove-mist separation. ENVIRONMENT: bamboo grove, masked rival, still pond. AUDIO (Block A, in-frame diegetic): a soft wind bed, bamboo creak and leaf-rustle, a distant birdcall, a faint blade-ring as it settles at 3.2s; score dizi and wind only underneath. DURATION: 6.0s. FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180°, natural blur on leaves and tassel. LIP-SYNC: none, lips set. NEGATIVE: no cuts, no second camera move, no clash yet, no blood, no extra people, no warping, no text.
