IDENTITY LOCK: Aira [paste identity-lock reference sheet here], continuity from Frame 1. Scene: the engage — Aira launches into a gliding lunge across the path, jian thrusting toward the rival's blade, robe and leaves streaming behind, the first exchange beginning. WARDROBE: martial robe pressed by the lunge, sleeves rippling. FOOTWEAR: boots gliding off the moss-stone, rear leg extended. EYE COLOUR: amber-brown, narrowed and fixed on the rival blade. ACCESSORIES: forearm wraps, jade ring, cuff, scabbard, tassel streaming. HAIR: braid tail snapping straight. MAKEUP: matte, gold-lit. HANDS & NAILS: firm extended grip driving the thrust, wrist aligned. EXPRESSION: determination — narrowed focus-eyes, clenched jaw, brow knit, nostrils flaring on the lunge breath, lips pressed flat. BODY POSTURE & WEIGHT: wound forward over the lunge, weight driving onto the lead boot, shoulders behind the thrust. SPATIAL LOGIC: foreground streaming leaves and the thrusting blade-tip; midground Aira lunging; background the rival raising guard, bamboo, mist. LIGHTING: KEY god-rays; RIM bright edge on braid, sleeve, and blade; FILL jade bounce. FRAMING: ASPECT 9:16, tight medium, slight Dutch, blade leading the upper third. SUBJECT FRAMING & POSITION: Framed as tight medium, slight Dutch, blade leading the upper third, on a canted Dutch angle. The upper body fills the frame, chest-to-head. Her body is alive and committed to the motion, in dynamically angled to camera. Camera distance follows the shot size, holding the action sharp. The beat: the engage — Aira launches into a gliding lunge across the path, jian thrusting toward the rival's blade, robe and leaves streaming behind, the first exchange beginning. Her pose reads as wound forward over the lunge, weight driving onto the lead boot, shoulders behind the thrust — gaze fixed dead ahead, pupils tight on the vanishing point; head held level and steady through the lurch; both shoulders driving forward then pinned; the RIGHT hand clamping the wheel or grip while the LEFT hand locks beside it, tendons raised; torso thrown back into the seat by explosive acceleration, core locked; waist braced, hips pinned; the RIGHT leg stamping the throttle while the LEFT braces on the floor; both feet committed, weight slamming rearward; braid whipping straight back as speed builds. ORIENTATION LOCK — preserve this exact left-to-right composition; do not mirror, flip or invert the frame. LENS + DOF + BOKEH: 50mm-equivalent, f/2.8, sharp on the blade and her, background to motion-bokeh. ATMOSPHERE: streaming leaves, mist-trail, lens-fleck. COLOR GRADE: jade-and-gold, deep shadow, punchy contrast, leaf-bloom. TEXTURE & MICRO-DETAIL: skin and wrap-weave, blade-edge glint, robe-fabric flow, drifting leaves. NEGATIVE: no extra limbs, no blood, no warped blade, no facial blur, no logos, no oversaturation, no sexualization, no enumeration, no text.

FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180°, strong background streak, crisp blade. LIP-SYNC: none; a lunge-breath. NEGATIVE: no second cut, no floaty motion, no blood, no warped face, no text, no extra duelists.

WARDROBE: robe taut in the bind, sleeves rippling. FOOTWEAR: boots planted and pivoting on the moss-stone. EYE COLOUR: amber-brown, hard and level over the locked blades. ACCESSORIES: wraps, ring, cuff, tassel snapping. HAIR: braid flicking. MAKEUP: matte, dew-sheen on the jaw. HANDS & NAILS: two-handed pressure on the hilt, forearm cords raised. EXPRESSION (, hardened): locked-in cold focus — thousand-yard lock over the blades, jaw set, controlled breathing, first chest-heave of exertion. BODY POSTURE & WEIGHT: rooted and driving into the bind, weight centred low, shoulders pressing the blade. SPATIAL LOGIC: foreground the locked sparking edges; midground the two duelists in the bind; background scattering leaves, bamboo, mist. LIGHTING: KEY god-rays three-quarter; RIM bright edge on both blades and braid; FILL jade bounce. FRAMING: ASPECT 9:16, dynamic two-shot, blades crossed at frame-centre. SUBJECT FRAMING & POSITION: Framed as dynamic two-shot, blades crossed at frame-centre, on a dynamic action angle. The figure is framed for motion. Her body is alive and committed to the motion, in dynamically angled to camera. Camera distance follows the shot size, holding the action sharp. The beat: the full clash — a fast bind of blades, edges locked and sparking faint jade light, the two duelists chest to chest in a wuxia exchange, leaves scattering from the speed. Her pose reads as rooted and driving into the bind, weight centred low, shoulders pressing the blade — gaze drilling down the road in a thousand-yard lock; head dead-level and steady; both shoulders driving and committed forward; the RIGHT hand firm on the wheel with micro-corrections in the wrist while the LEFT controls the opposite rim; torso forward-leaning into the speed, weight centred and driving; waist engaged, hips set into the seat; the RIGHT leg steady on the throttle with the heel down while the LEFT braces; both feet planted and controlling, weight centred; braid streaming horizontal in the slipstream. ORIENTATION LOCK — preserve this exact left-to-right composition; do not mirror, flip or invert the frame. LENS + DOF + BOKEH: 85mm-equivalent, f/4, sharp on the bind, grove to bokeh. ATMOSPHERE: scattering leaves, faint jade blade-spark, mist. COLOR GRADE: jade-and-gold, high micro-contrast, spark-bloom. TEXTURE & MICRO-DETAIL: skin and wraps, blade-on-blade scuff and spark, robe-weave, leaf-edges. NEGATIVE: no blood, no warped blades, no facial blur, no logos, no oversaturation, no sexualization, no enumeration, no text.

EXPRESSION: triumph and relief — tension easing from the brow, a long visible exhale, a brief fierce close-lipped smile, chest still heaving. BODY POSTURE & WEIGHT: tall and composed, weight grounded, shoulders dropping back, a victor's stillness. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

EXPRESSION TRANSITION: into into — hard focus melts to a heaving-chest release at 2.8s, resolving into eased-brow triumph and a brief fierce smile by 5.2s. LIGHTING: warm god-ray key, bright rim, fading qi practicals. ENVIRONMENT: settling grove, kneeling rival, still pond. AUDIO (Block A): the wind and leaf-rustle returning, falling leaves, a distant birdcall, a long exhale at 2.8s, a final faint blade-shimmer as the qi fades; score lands a soft erhu-and-bell resolve then tails to the opening dizi for the loop. DURATION: 6.0s. FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180°, gentle blur on the rise, crisp on the face. LIP-SYNC: none; an exhale and a closed-lip smile. NEGATIVE: no blood, no cut, no second move, no extra people up close, no warped geometry, no text or watermark.

## Concept 12 — Sandstorm Spear Dance

Lane: Weapon · a spear duel through the ruins of a desert city in a rising sandstorm.

Format: Seven-frame vertical action reel — one continuous spear duel through wind-scoured sandstone ruins as a sandstorm builds at golden hour, rendered as seven seeded keyframes, roughly twenty-one seconds.

Hero Weapon: A long ash-shafted spear (qiang) with a leaf-shaped steel head and a crimson horsehair tassel; the head catches amber light and the tassel snaps in the wind; realistic wear only — sand-scour, a few honest nicks — never bloodied, contact landing on the rival's shaft, guard, and stone only.

Rival Element: A veiled spear-fighter with a matching qiang, trading sweeping pole exchanges; takes only shaft-scuffs and is unbalanced and disarmed, never wounded.

Setting: A half-buried sandstone city at golden hour — toppled colonnades, carved arches, drifting dunes — as a towering ochre sandstorm wall rolls in, wind tearing veils of sand through the ruins, the low sun burning amber through the haze. Each area of the frame intentionally composed, pristine and luxurious, with true foreground-to-background layering and immersive environmental depth. Architecture in fine premium materials with an immaculate finish.

Wardrobe: A fitted sand-and-bronze desert combat wrap with layered articulated panels, a wind-scarf at the collar, bound sleeves and sashed trousers over shin-wraps — full-coverage, modest, desert-warrior-coded.

Footwear: Bronze-buckled desert combat boots with wrapped ankles and grippy soles, sure on shifting sand and stone.

Eye Colour: Warm amber-brown (consistent locked identity), blazing in the amber light, narrowed against the sand.

Accessories: A wind-scarf (drawn up over nose and mouth from Frame 4 as the storm bites), forearm wraps, a bronze arm-ring, the thin titanium wrist cuff, the crimson-tasseled spear.

Hair: The same tight low braid, bound with bronze wire, tail snapping in the wind.

Makeup: Matte sun-burnished complexion, defined brow, cool eye, neutral lip; sand-dust and sweat sheen permitted.

Movement (the one impossible move): At the climax of a spinning spear-sweep, the torn sand does not scatter — it gathers into a towering, slowly-turning vortex of golden sand that circles her like a living column, suspended and shot through with amber light, then disperses on the wind.

Concept Line: "She dances the spear through the storm — and the desert rises to spin with her."

Duration: ~21 seconds · 7 frames · 24fps · 180° shutter.

Mood: Epic, scouring, elemental, sublime.

Composition: Toppled-column verticals, dune leading-lines, blowing-sand foregrounds, the two fighters raked across thirds; intercut a guard-up two-shot, a pole-clash, a spinning-sweep hero beat, a sand-vortex reveal, and a hero stillness. Rich layered foreground-to-background depth, elegant spatial hierarchy and clean visual flow (original framing and angle preserved).

Grade: Amber-and-bronze, deep ruin-shadow, ochre haze, glowing gold for the vortex magic, rich blacks, controlled bloom on sand and sun. Premium editorial grade — rich clean colour, luminous mid-tones, deep-but-clean blacks, controlled elegant saturation, no faded or muddy tones, natural true skin preserved.

Identity Safety: Aira rendered entirely from the locked reference sheet; adult, 25; no physical or ethnic enumeration; full-coverage wardrobe; stylized spearwork — contact lands on shaft, guard, and stone only, no blood, no wounds.

### 🎚️ Master Soundtrack & Sound-Design Brief — Audio Block B (outside the prompts)

Role: The continuous score and sound-design bed under the in-frame diegetic audio across all seven frames, binding them into one twenty-one-second piece. Genre & palette: Epic desert-cinematic — frame-drums and dohола percussion, a droning oud and duduk, a soaring vocal ululation, a shimmering bell-and-string theme reserved for the sand-vortex magic, and wind-and-sand ambience. Tempo & key: 104 BPM, A Phrygian dominant, 4/4, locked so sweeps and clashes land on the accent. Instrumentation by layer: low frame-drum and sub (footwork and lunges), darbuka runs (the exchanges), oud and duduk (the lyrical line), soaring vocal and shimmering strings (the sand-vortex magic), granular wind-and-sand-hiss texture (atmosphere). Dynamic arc: Frame 1 spare and droning (duduk and wind only); Frames 2–3 frame-drums and darbuka enter with the engage and clash; Frame 4 a vocal stab as the storm and the rival's attack hit; Frame 5 a darbuka riser into the spinning sweep; Frame 6 the bell-and-string theme blooms with a soaring vocal as the vortex rises; Frame 7 a resolving oud phrase tailing to the opening duduk for a perfect loop. Hits & transitions: a frame-drum hit on the first lunge (F2), ringing shaft-clack accents (F3–F4), a darbuka riser into the sweep (F5), a beat-dropout and 250ms suspended silence as the vortex gathers (F5→F6), a vocal-and-string swell through the spinning sand (F6), a soft oud-and-bell resolve on the hero stillness (F7). Mix: score about 6 dB under the wind and shaft-foley; sidechain the bed to the biggest clacks; wide-stereo vocal and strings, mono-tight sub; tape warmth, vast ruin-reverb on the bells. Loop point: the closing duduk matches bar one.

MASTER AUDIO: a 42-second custom cinematic score (7 clips × 6s) composed in self-resolving ~6-second phrases, so it can be trimmed to the reel’s assembled length at any phrase boundary without a hard cut — it always resolves, never stops dead or loops audibly. Built on deep trailer percussion, a driving synth bass and brass swells — premium, cinematic and emotionally catching, never generic stock or empty ambience. It opens immediately on a low brass-and-sub impact (no fade-in) so the mood lands within the first second. Emotional arc: an airy intimate open across the first beats, a rising shimmer of anticipation toward frame 5, the warm emotional peak on the signature move — At the climax of a spinning spear-sweep, the torn sand does not scatter, then a graceful resolve back toward the opening for an invisible loop. SPOKEN reel — the score opens space for the spoken line at its beat and ducks gently beneath the voice, then re-blooms after. Layered as one system — lead melody, warm sub for body, taut percussive tension as atmosphere, restrained percussion, and event impacts reserved for the major beats, each tuned to the score’s own key so every hit reads as the music itself expanding, never an added sound effect.

WARDROBE: wrap settling, lit by the golden vortex, panels glowing. FOOTWEAR: boots planted from the sweep. EYE COLOUR: amber-brown above the lowering scarf, blazing, the vortex mirrored in the iris. ACCESSORIES: scarf easing down, wraps, arm-ring, cuff, tassel lifting in the vortex. HAIR: braid lifting, edged in golden light. MAKEUP: matte, golden glow across the brow. HANDS & NAILS: sure grip, spear raised, one hand opening in wonder. EXPRESSION (into ): awe breaking into defiant thrill — brows lifting and eyes widening at the towering vortex, then a fierce exhilarated grin (scarf lowering to reveal it), eyes bright and reckless. BODY POSTURE & WEIGHT: weight settling from the spin, chest lifting, shoulders opening with exhilaration. SPATIAL LOGIC: foreground the curling base of the sand-vortex; midground Aira at its eye with the raised spear; background the frozen rival and ruins seen through the spinning gold. LIGHTING: KEY the self-luminous golden vortex casting warm light onto her face; RIM glowing edge; FILL ambient bloom. FRAMING: ASPECT 9:16, hero three-quarter, the vortex spiralling around her toward the spear. SUBJECT FRAMING & POSITION: Framed as hero three-quarter, the vortex spiralling around her toward the spear, on a dynamic action angle. The figure is framed for motion. Her body is alive and committed to the motion, in a three-quarter view to camera. Camera distance follows the shot size, holding the action sharp. The beat: the magic blooms — the torn sand gathers into a towering, slowly-turning vortex of golden sand that circles Aira like a living column, suspended and shot through with amber light, the unbalanced rival frozen at its edge. Her pose reads as weight settling from the spin, chest lifting, shoulders opening with exhilaration — gaze level and reading the scene with cold focus; head steady, turned to the action; both shoulders settled and squared, alive with breath; the RIGHT arm poised and ready with the hand relaxed but set; the LEFT arm easy and counter-balanced at her side; torso upright and composed in living stillness; waist long with the hips squared, weight carried on the back foot; the RIGHT leg bearing the load with a soft knee while the LEFT braces with one heel just lifted; both feet planted and grounded, weight back and ready to spring; hair alive in the wind, sealed and controlled. ORIENTATION LOCK — preserve this exact left-to-right composition; do not mirror, flip or invert the frame. LENS + DOF + BOKEH: 85mm-equivalent, f/2.8, spinning sand in luminous bokeh, sharp on her face. ATMOSPHERE: a towering suspended sand-vortex, drifting golden motes. COLOR GRADE: maximal warm gold glow, deep ruin-shadow for contrast, gentle bloom on the vortex. TEXTURE & MICRO-DETAIL: skin, fine grain-streaks in the turning vortex, glints riding the spiral, sand on the wrap catching glow. NEGATIVE: no extra people beyond the frozen rival, no blood, no warped face, no garish clipping, no logos, no oversaturation beyond intended bloom, no sexualization, no enumeration, no text.

### Frame 6 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT
STRICT COLOUR & RENDER MANDATE (must be followed exactly; this governs render QUALITY only and must NOT change the composition, camera, lens, framing, wardrobe, action or identity specified above): render the colours already specified at their richest, freshest, cleanest and most premium — crisp, vivid and luminous with elegant, controlled saturation; never dull, faded, washed-out, muddy, grey or flat. Hold deep-but-clean blacks, luminous full mid-tones and beautiful highlight roll-off with high dynamic range and no blown highlights. Wherever sky is visible, render it richly graded and cinematic — never empty, white or overexposed. Wherever water is visible, render it crystal-clear with deep sea-blue and turquoise gradients, clean caustics and sparkling reflections — never grey or murky (unless the concept explicitly specifies dark / ink / obsidian water, which stays deep, glassy and clean). Protect natural, true skin tone; never push saturation onto the face. Keep the image immaculately clean and premium with clear figure-to-ground separation so the hero reads first; honour the concept's intended dominant surface and full-frame coverage, and do NOT reveal unwanted structural edges, seams, side-walls or empty dead space that break the intended composition. Photoreal, award-winning editorial finish — no AI artifacts, plastic or waxy skin, dead eyes, malformed hands or unfinished backgrounds.

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: Hero three-quarter reveal shot. CAMERA MOVEMENT: one move only — a slow rise-and-settle (crane up) revealing the towering vortex. LENS: 85mm-equivalent, f/2.8. PHYSICS: the torn sand defies the wind, gathering and slowing into a towering column-vortex that turns slowly around her, holding its form and refracting amber light, then loses cohesion and disperses on the wind; grit streams along the spiral. SUBJECT + OBJECT ACTION (timecoded): 0.0–2.0s as Aira sets from the sweep, the blasting sand slows and begins to gather into a turning column around her (sand spiralling inward); 2.0–4.0s it rises into a towering golden vortex shot through with amber light, the rival frozen at its edge, her scarf lowering as she looks up; 4.0–6.0s the vortex turns once more and begins to disperse on the wind, golden motes streaming off. EXPRESSION TRANSITION: into into — strained effort releases into widened-eye awe at 2.4s as the vortex rises, blooming into a fierce grin by 4.8s. LIGHTING: self-luminous golden vortex up-lighting her face, glowing rim. ENVIRONMENT: the desert risen into a spinning column of light. AUDIO (Block A): the storm-howl drops as the vortex gathers, a deep rising sand-rush and a shimmering swell from 2.0s, a soft fierce exhale at 4.8s; score blooms the bell-and-string theme with a soaring vocal. DURATION: 6.0s. FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180°, silky blur on the rise, crisp spiralling sand. LIP-SYNC: none; a breath of wonder. NEGATIVE: no cut, no second move, no cartoonish glow, no blood, no warped spear, no text.

---

## Concept 13 — Volcanic Forge Fire-Fist

Lane: Unarmed combat · a hand-to-hand fight on the bridges of a vast volcanic forge.

Format: Eight-frame vertical action reel — one continuous unarmed fight across iron catwalks above a lava-forge cavern, rendered as eight seeded keyframes, roughly twenty-four seconds.

Hero Element: No weapon — Aira fights with mind-blowing martial choreography: precise punches, elbows, and a signature spinning kick. Her wrapped fists glow faintly with reflected forge-light; realistic wear only — scuffed wraps, soot, sweat — never bloodied, contact landing on the opponent's guard, pads, and gear only.

Opponent: A single armored brawler in heat-blackened forge-gear that Aira fights hand-to-hand — no weapons, taking impacts on guard and pads, driven back and downed, never wounded.

Setting: A colossal subterranean forge-cavern — iron catwalks and chain-bridges over a churning lava lake, glowing crucibles, hanging anchor-chains, roaring bellows, sparks raining from above, the air shimmering with heat. Every zone of the frame intentionally designed, immaculately clean and premium, with layered foreground, midground and background depth and rich environmental storytelling. Water rendered crystal-clear in rich sea-blue and turquoise gradients, with beautiful caustics and clean sparkling reflections.

Wardrobe: A fitted charcoal-and-ember combat suit with heat-resistant articulated panels, a high collar and bound forearms, sashed waist over shin-guards — full-coverage, modest, martial-coded.

Footwear: Black combat boots with ember-orange soles, reinforced toe, grippy on hot iron — important, since the fight is foot-driven.

Eye Colour: Warm amber-brown (consistent locked identity), lit molten by the forge, fixed on the opponent.

Accessories: Hand-wraps (knuckles taped), a slim impact vest, the thin titanium wrist cuff; no helmet (catwalk fight).

Hair: The same tight low braid, soot-flecked, tail snapping with each strike.

Makeup: Matte heat-flushed complexion, defined brow, cool eye, neutral lip; soot and sweat sheen permitted.

Movement (the one impossible move): At the apex of her signature spinning kick, the rising sparks and embers do not scatter — they swarm into a glowing orbital cloud that wraps her fists and the kicking heel like living fire, tracing her strike in molten light, then disperse upward as cooling motes.

Concept Line: "She fights over the fire — and the forge itself rises to her fists."

Duration: ~24 seconds · 8 frames · 24fps · 180° shutter.

Mood: Molten, thunderous, ferocious, sublime.

Composition: Catwalk leading-lines, chain verticals, ember-rain foregrounds, the two fighters raked across thirds; intercut a bridge two-shot, a strike-exchange, a 360-kick hero beat, an ember-swarm reveal, a catwalk near-miss, and a hero stance. Rich layered foreground-to-background depth, elegant spatial hierarchy and clean visual flow (original framing and angle preserved).

Grade: Ember-orange and iron-black, molten core-glow, deep cavern shadow, incandescent gold for the ember magic, rich blacks, controlled bloom on lava and sparks. Premium editorial grade — rich clean colour, luminous mid-tones, deep-but-clean blacks, controlled elegant saturation, no faded or muddy tones, natural true skin preserved.

Identity Safety: Aira rendered entirely from the locked reference sheet; adult, 25; no physical or ethnic enumeration; full-coverage wardrobe; stylized unarmed combat — impacts land on guard, pads, and gear only, no gore, no weapons, no burns.

### 🎚️ Master Soundtrack & Sound-Design Brief — Audio Block B (outside the prompts)

Role: The continuous score and sound-design bed under the in-frame diegetic audio across all eight frames, binding them into one twenty-four-second piece. Genre & palette: Heavy industrial-hybrid — anvil-and-metal percussion, a sub-heavy distorted bass, a tense brass-and-choir ostinato, a soaring molten-choir theme reserved for the ember-swarm magic, and forge-and-fire ambience. Tempo & key: 128 BPM, C minor, 4/4, locked so strikes land on the downbeat. Instrumentation by layer: sub and distorted bass (the forge's mass), anvil-hits and taiko (the strikes), tense brass-and-choir ostinato (the fight), soaring molten-choir and bells (the ember magic), granular fire-roar-and-bellows texture (atmosphere). Dynamic arc: Frame 1 sparse and roaring (drone and forge only); Frames 2–3 anvil-percussion and bass enter with the engage and clash; Frame 4 a brass stab on the hazard; Frame 5 an anvil riser into the 360 kick with one huge hit on contact; Frame 6 the molten-choir theme blooms over a riser as the embers swarm; Frame 7 a half-time breakdown for the catwalk near-miss; Frame 8 full triumphant tutti tailing to the opening drone for a perfect loop. Hits & transitions: sub-drop on the first strike (F2), a brass stab on the hazard (F4), an anvil riser into the spin (F5), one massive impact-hit and a 250ms beat-dropout on the kick's contact (F5), a molten-choir swell as the embers swarm (F6), an impact boom and brief near-silence at the catwalk near-miss (F7), a brass-and-bell stinger on the hero stance (F8). Mix: score about 6 dB under the forge and combat-impacts; sidechain the bed to the biggest hits; wide-stereo brass and choir, mono-tight sub; tape saturation, vast cavern-reverb on the choir. Loop point: the closing drone matches bar one.

MASTER AUDIO: a 48-second custom cinematic score (8 clips × 6s) composed in self-resolving ~6-second phrases, so it can be trimmed to the reel’s assembled length at any phrase boundary without a hard cut — it always resolves, never stops dead or loops audibly. Built on deep trailer percussion, a driving synth bass and brass swells — premium, cinematic and emotionally catching, never generic stock or empty ambience. It opens immediately on a low brass-and-sub impact (no fade-in) so the mood lands within the first second. Emotional arc: an airy intimate open across the first beats, a rising shimmer of anticipation toward frame 5, the warm emotional peak on the signature move — At the apex of her signature spinning kick, the rising sparks and embers do not scatter, then a graceful resolve back toward the opening for an invisible loop. SPOKEN reel — the score opens space for the spoken line at its beat and ducks gently beneath the voice, then re-blooms after. Layered as one system — lead melody, warm sub for body, taut percussive tension as atmosphere, restrained percussion, and event impacts reserved for the major beats, each tuned to the score’s own key so every hit reads as the music itself expanding, never an added sound effect.

Keep the image immaculately clean and premium with clear figure-to-ground separation so the hero reads first; honour the concept's intended dominant surface and full-frame coverage, and do NOT reveal unwanted structural edges, seams, side-walls or empty dead space that break the intended composition. Photoreal, award-winning editorial finish — no AI artifacts, plastic or waxy skin, dead eyes, malformed hands or unfinished backgrounds.

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: Heroic low-angle finale shot. CAMERA MOVEMENT: one move only — a slow push-in and slight rise settling on Aira. LENS: 35mm-equivalent, f/4. PHYSICS: the last embers cool and drift upward with convection; Aira holds a tall final stance against residual momentum; the ember-glow fades; the forge settles to a steady roar. SUBJECT + OBJECT ACTION (timecoded): 0.0–2.0s Aira sets her final fighting stance on the far span as the brawler stays downed (embers cooling, drifting up); 2.0–4.0s she stands tall and squares up, fists unclenching, braid loosening; 4.0–6.0s she exhales a long breath and a brief fierce smile breaks as the last embers wink out around her. EXPRESSION TRANSITION: into into — hard focus melts to a heaving-chest release at 2.8s, resolving into eased-brow triumph and a brief fierce smile by 5.2s. LIGHTING: warm lava-glow key, ember rim, fading ember practicals. ENVIRONMENT: the settling forge, downed brawler, broken bridge. AUDIO (Block A): the forge-roar settling to a steady bed, hissing cooling metal, a chain-creak, a long exhale at 2.8s, a final faint ember-shimmer as the glow fades; score lands a brass-and-bell stinger then tails to the opening drone for the loop. DURATION: 6.0s. FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180°, gentle blur on the rise, crisp on the face. LIP-SYNC: none; an exhale and a closed-lip smile. NEGATIVE: no gore, no weapons, no burns, no cut, no second move, no extra people up close, no warped geometry, no text or watermark.

---

## Concept 14 — Storm-Cliff Lightning Katana

Lane: Weapon · a katana duel on a storm-lashed clifftop in driving rain and lightning.

Format: Eight-frame vertical action reel — one continuous sword duel on a black sea-cliff during a night thunderstorm, rendered as eight seeded keyframes, roughly twenty-four seconds.

Hero Weapon: A slender katana with a deep-blue ito-wrapped hilt and a mirror-polished blade that throws lightning-reflections; realistic wear only — rain-beads, a few honest nicks — never bloodied, contact landing on the rival's blade, guard, and stone only.

Rival Element: A hooded swordfighter with a matching katana, trading clean kenjutsu exchanges; takes only blade-scuffs and is disarmed, never wounded.

Setting: A black basalt sea-cliff at night in a violent thunderstorm — driving rain, a torii gate and a lone wind-bent pine, the sea smashing far below, lightning ripping the sky and strobing the duel in blue-white flashes. Each area of the frame intentionally composed, pristine and luxurious, with true foreground-to-background layering and immersive environmental depth. Water rendered crystal-clear in rich sea-blue and turquoise gradients, with beautiful caustics and clean sparkling reflections. Sky richly graded and cinematic with elegant clouds and premium light, never flat or empty.

Wardrobe: A fitted indigo-and-steel martial robe with a layered storm-cloak, articulated bound sleeves, high collar and sashed hakama over shin-wraps — full-coverage, modest, kenjutsu-coded, rain-soaked.

Footwear: Black split-toe martial boots (tabi-style) with grippy soles, sure on the wet basalt.

Eye Colour: Warm amber-brown (consistent locked identity), flashing blue-white in the lightning, fixed on the rival blade.

Accessories: A storm-cloak (streaming in the wind), forearm wraps, a steel guard-ring, the thin titanium wrist cuff, the indigo-hilted katana and saya.

Hair: The same tight low braid, rain-slicked, tail whipping in the gale.

Makeup: Matte rain-wet complexion, defined brow, cool eye, neutral lip; rain-sheen permitted.

Movement (the one impossible move): As a lightning bolt strikes her raised blade, the bolt does not vanish — it freezes in mid-air into a vast crystalline arc of forked blue-white light hanging over the cliff, branching and still, mirrored along her katana, then shatters into rain.

Concept Line: "She duels in the storm — and catches the lightning on her blade."

Duration: ~24 seconds · 8 frames · 24fps · 180° shutter.

Mood: Tempestuous, electric, austere, sublime.

Composition: Torii and pine verticals, cliff-edge leading-lines, rain-streak foregrounds, the two duelists raked across thirds; intercut a guard-up two-shot, a blade-clash, a spinning-cut hero beat, a frozen-lightning reveal, a cliff-edge near-miss, and a hero stillness. Premium foreground, midground and background layering with strong three-dimensional depth and clean visual balance (original framing preserved).

Grade: Indigo-and-steel, blue-white lightning strobe, deep storm-black, glowing electric-cyan for the frozen-lightning magic, rich blacks, controlled bloom on rain and flashes. World-class editorial colour — rich and vivid yet clean, luminous mid-tones, deep-but-clean blacks, controlled saturation, nothing washed-out, natural skin protected.

Identity Safety: Aira rendered entirely from the locked reference sheet; adult, 25; no physical or ethnic enumeration; full-coverage wardrobe; stylized bladework — contact lands on blade, guard, and stone only, no blood, no wounds.

### 🎚️ Master Soundtrack & Sound-Design Brief — Audio Block B (outside the prompts)

Role: The continuous score and sound-design bed under the in-frame diegetic audio across all eight frames, binding them into one twenty-four-second piece. Genre & palette: Dark epic-cinematic — taiko and storm-percussion, a sub-heavy drone, a tense low-string ostinato, a soaring shakuhachi-and-choir theme reserved for the frozen-lightning magic, and rain-and-thunder ambience. Tempo & key: 110 BPM, F-sharp minor, 4/4, locked so clashes and thunder land on the downbeat. Instrumentation by layer: sub and storm-drone (the tempest), taiko and toms (footwork and cuts), low-string ostinato (the exchanges), shakuhachi and wordless choir (the frozen-lightning magic), granular rain-and-wind texture (atmosphere). Dynamic arc: Frame 1 spare and storm-driven (drone and rain only); Frames 2–3 taiko and strings enter with the engage and clash; Frame 4 a thunder-stab on the rival's attack; Frame 5 a taiko riser into the spinning cut; Frame 6 the shakuhachi-and-choir theme blooms over a riser as the lightning freezes; Frame 7 a half-time breakdown for the cliff-edge near-miss; Frame 8 a resolving choir phrase tailing to the opening drone for a perfect loop. Hits & transitions: a taiko hit on the first lunge (F2), ringing blade-clang accents (F3–F4), a taiko riser into the cut (F5), a thunder-crack and 300ms suspended silence as the lightning freezes (F5→F6), a choir-swell through the frozen arc (F6), a thunder-boom and brief near-silence at the cliff-edge near-miss (F7), a soft choir-and-bell resolve on the hero stillness (F8). Mix: score about 6 dB under the rain and blade-foley; sidechain the bed to the biggest thunder and clangs; wide-stereo choir and strings, mono-tight sub; tape warmth, vast cliff-reverb on the choir. Loop point: the closing drone matches bar one.

MASTER AUDIO: a 48-second custom cinematic score (8 clips × 6s) composed in self-resolving ~6-second phrases, so it can be trimmed to the reel’s assembled length at any phrase boundary without a hard cut — it always resolves, never stops dead or loops audibly. Built on deep trailer percussion, a driving synth bass and brass swells — premium, cinematic and emotionally catching, never generic stock or empty ambience. It opens immediately on a low brass-and-sub impact (no fade-in) so the mood lands within the first second. Emotional arc: an airy intimate open across the first beats, a rising shimmer of anticipation toward frame 5, the warm emotional peak on the signature move — As a lightning bolt strikes her raised blade, the bolt does not vanish, then a graceful resolve back toward the opening for an invisible loop. SPOKEN reel — the score opens space for the spoken line at its beat and ducks gently beneath the voice, then re-blooms after. Layered as one system — lead melody, warm sub for body, taut percussive tension as atmosphere, restrained percussion, and event impacts reserved for the major beats, each tuned to the score’s own key so every hit reads as the music itself expanding, never an added sound effect.

TEXTURE & MICRO-DETAIL: pores and rain-beads, soaked robe weave, mirror blade with faint nicks, wet-basalt and torii-grain detail. NEGATIVE: no extra people beyond the rival, no facial blur, no distorted hands, no blood, no logos or text, no warped blade geometry, no oversaturation, no sexualization, no physical or ethnic enumeration, no watermark.

Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: Hero three-quarter reveal shot. CAMERA MOVEMENT: one move only — a slow rise-and-settle (crane up) revealing the frozen-lightning arc. LENS: 85mm-equivalent, f/2.8. PHYSICS: a lightning bolt strikes the raised blade and, instead of vanishing, freezes into a vast crystalline forked arc that hangs branching and still over the cliff, mirrored along the katana; charged rain hangs suspended; then the arc loses cohesion and shatters into falling rain. SUBJECT + OBJECT ACTION (timecoded): 0.0–2.0s a lightning bolt strikes Aira's raised blade and halts mid-air, beginning to crystallize (the bolt freezing); 2.0–4.0s it sets into a vast still arc of forked blue-white light over the cliff, the rival frozen below, the rain hanging suspended; 4.0–6.0s the frozen arc trembles and shatters into falling rain, light raining down. EXPRESSION TRANSITION: into into — strained effort releases into parted-lip awe at 2.4s as the lightning freezes, blooming into a wild grin by 4.8s. LIGHTING: self-luminous frozen lightning up-lighting her face, glowing rim. ENVIRONMENT: the storm halted around a single frozen bolt. AUDIO (Block A): the thunder-crack stretching and freezing into a sustained crystalline ring as the bolt sets from 2.0s, the rain dropping to near-silence, a soft delighted exhale at 4.8s; score blooms the shakuhachi-and-choir theme over a riser. DURATION: 6.0s. FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180°, silky blur on the rise, crisp frozen arc. LIP-SYNC: none; a breath of wonder. NEGATIVE: no cut, no second move, no cartoonish glow, no blood, no warped blade, no text.

## Concept 15 — Bullet-Train Rooftop Fight

Lane: Hybrid unarmed combat · a hand-to-hand fight on the roof of a bullet train at full speed.

Format: Eight-frame vertical action reel — one continuous unarmed fight along the roof of a speeding bullet train across a sunset viaduct, rendered as eight seeded keyframes, roughly twenty-four seconds.

Hero Element: No weapon — Aira fights with mind-blowing martial choreography against a 300-km/h slipstream: bladed hand-strikes, sweeps, and a signature spinning kick, every move braced against the wind. Her taped hands and braced stance read the gale; realistic wear only — wind-torn sleeves, no blood — contact landing on the opponent's guard and gear only.

Opponent: A single wind-goggled agent in a streamlined bodysuit that Aira fights hand-to-hand — no weapons, taking impacts on guard and gear, driven back along the roof and downed, never wounded.

Setting: The roof of a white-and-cobalt bullet train at full speed across a soaring viaduct at golden hour — rice-paddies and a far mountain blurring past, the slipstream screaming, signal-gantries flashing overhead, the low sun strobing between pylons. Each area of the frame intentionally composed, pristine and luxurious, with true foreground-to-background layering and immersive environmental depth.

Wardrobe: A fitted slate-and-cobalt aero combat suit with low-drag articulated panels, a snug hood-down collar, bound forearms and a sashed waist over shin-guards — full-coverage, modest, aero-martial-coded, snapping in the wind.

Footwear: Black magnetic-grip combat boots with cobalt soles, reinforced toe — critical for footing on the smooth roof at speed.

Eye Colour: Warm amber-brown (consistent locked identity), watering-bright in the slipstream, fixed on the opponent.

Accessories: Slim aero-goggles pushed up on the brow, taped hands, a low-profile harness-vest, the thin titanium wrist cuff.

Hair: The same tight low braid, streaming dead-flat backward in the slipstream.

Makeup: Matte wind-bitten complexion, defined brow, cool eye, neutral lip; wind-flush and sweat sheen permitted.

Movement (the one impossible move): At the apex of her signature spinning kick, the screaming slipstream becomes visible — the wind-shear peels into long glowing ribbons of golden light that spiral the length of the train and wrap her kick, tracing the airflow, then stream off the tail and fade.

Concept Line: "She fights the wind at full speed — and the slipstream turns to light around her."

Duration: ~24 seconds · 8 frames · 24fps · 180° shutter.

Mood: Screaming-fast, kinetic, vertiginous, sublime.

Composition: Train-roof leading-lines rushing to vanishing point, gantry verticals, slipstream-blur foregrounds, the two fighters raked across thirds; intercut a roof two-shot, a strike-exchange, a 360-kick hero beat, a light-ribbon reveal, a gantry near-miss, and a hero stance. Premium foreground, midground and background layering with strong three-dimensional depth and clean visual balance (original framing preserved).

Grade: Cobalt-and-gold, sunset warmth, motion-blurred landscape, incandescent gold for the light-ribbon magic, rich blacks, controlled bloom on the sun and ribbons. Premium editorial grade — rich clean colour, luminous mid-tones, deep-but-clean blacks, controlled elegant saturation, no faded or muddy tones, natural true skin preserved.

Identity Safety: Aira rendered entirely from the locked reference sheet; adult, 25; no physical or ethnic enumeration; full-coverage wardrobe; stylized unarmed combat — impacts land on guard and gear only, no gore, no weapons.

### 🎚️ Master Soundtrack & Sound-Design Brief — Audio Block B (outside the prompts)

Role: The continuous score and sound-design bed under the in-frame diegetic audio across all eight frames, binding them into one twenty-four-second piece. Genre & palette: High-velocity electronic-orchestral — driving four-on-the-floor kick and rail-rhythm percussion, a pulsing synth-bass arpeggio, a tense string-and-brass ostinato, a soaring synth-and-choir theme reserved for the light-ribbon magic, and slipstream-and-rail ambience. Tempo & key: 140 BPM, A minor, 4/4, locked so strikes and rail-clacks land on the beat. Instrumentation by layer: synth-sub and rail-thrum (the train's speed), punchy kick and snare (the strikes), pulsing arpeggio and string ostinato (the fight), soaring synth-lead and wordless choir (the light-ribbon magic), granular wind-roar texture (atmosphere). Dynamic arc: Frame 1 sparse and rushing (rail-thrum and wind only); Frames 2–3 kick and arpeggio enter with the engage and clash; Frame 4 a brass stab on the hazard; Frame 5 a riser into the 360 kick with one big hit on contact; Frame 6 the synth-and-choir theme blooms over a riser as the ribbons peel; Frame 7 a half-time breakdown for the gantry near-miss; Frame 8 full driving tutti tailing to the opening rail-thrum for a perfect loop. Hits & transitions: a kick-drop on the first strike (F2), a brass stab on the hazard (F4), a riser into the spin (F5), one big impact-hit and a 250ms beat-dropout on the kick's contact (F5), a synth-choir swell as the ribbons peel (F6), an impact boom and brief near-silence at the gantry near-miss (F7), a bright synth-and-bell stinger on the hero stance (F8). Mix: score about 6 dB under the slipstream and combat-impacts; sidechain the bed to the biggest hits; wide-stereo synth and choir, mono-tight sub; tape saturation, fast slap-delay on the lead. Loop point: the closing rail-thrum matches bar one.

MASTER AUDIO: a 48-second custom cinematic score (8 clips × 6s) composed in self-resolving ~6-second phrases, so it can be trimmed to the reel’s assembled length at any phrase boundary without a hard cut — it always resolves, never stops dead or loops audibly. Built on deep trailer percussion, a driving synth bass and brass swells — premium, cinematic and emotionally catching, never generic stock or empty ambience. It opens immediately on a low brass-and-sub impact (no fade-in) so the mood lands within the first second. Emotional arc: an airy intimate open across the first beats, a rising shimmer of anticipation toward frame 5, the warm emotional peak on the signature move — At the apex of her signature spinning kick, the screaming slipstream becomes visible, then a graceful resolve back toward the opening for an invisible loop. SPOKEN reel — the score opens space for the spoken line at its beat and ducks gently beneath the voice, then re-blooms after. Layered as one system — lead melody, warm sub for body, taut percussive tension as atmosphere, restrained percussion, and event impacts reserved for the major beats, each tuned to the score’s own key so every hit reads as the music itself expanding, never an added sound effect.

### Frame 1 of 8 — IMAGE PROMPT
STRICT COLOUR & RENDER MANDATE (must be followed exactly; this governs render QUALITY only and must NOT change the composition, camera, lens, framing, wardrobe, action or identity specified above): render the colours already specified at their richest, freshest, cleanest and most premium — crisp, vivid and luminous with elegant, controlled saturation; never dull, faded, washed-out, muddy, grey or flat. IDENTITY LOCK: Aira [paste identity-lock reference sheet here]. Scene: she crouches braced on the roof of the speeding bullet train, taped hands set low, reading the wind-goggled agent down the roofline as the viaduct and golden countryside scream past. WARDROBE: slate-and-cobalt aero suit, collar snug, panels snapping, wind-pressed. FOOTWEAR: magnetic-grip boots clamped to the smooth roof. EYE COLOUR: warm amber-brown, golden slipstream catchlight, gaze down the roof. ACCESSORIES: aero-goggles on the brow, taped hands, harness-vest, titanium cuff. BODY POSTURE & WEIGHT: weight low and braced into the slipstream, knees bent, shoulders set against the wind, living stillness — breath and braid-stream alive. AUDIO (Block A): a screaming gantry-and-tunnel-lip whoosh past at 1.8s, a dive-grunt, a roof-scrape slide, a caught gasp at 1.4s, the slipstream swallowed by tunnel-roar; score hits an impact boom then brief near-silence. DURATION: 6.0s. FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180°, intense blur on the slide and tunnel-lip. LIP-SYNC: none; a sharp gasp. NEGATIVE: no impact, no gore, no fall, no cut, no warped gantry, no text.

---

## Concept 16 — Helicopter-to-Car Canyon Transfer

Lane: Hybrid stunt · a mid-air transfer from a helicopter skid to a speeding car through a desert canyon.

Format: Eight-frame vertical action reel — one continuous aerial stunt as Aira drops from a low-flying helicopter onto a hero car racing a red-rock canyon, rendered as eight seeded keyframes, roughly twenty-four seconds.

Hero Element: A black low-flying helicopter and a matte-bronze rally-prepped muscle car racing the canyon floor — Aira hangs from the skid, releases, and drops to the roof of the moving car. Realistic stunt physics and stylized non-gory wear only — dust-scour, a dented roof-panel, no blood.

Hazard Element: A pursuing second vehicle and the closing canyon walls — obstacles only, no people harmed; damage lands on rock, dust, and the cars.

Setting: A vast red-rock slot-canyon at golden hour — towering striated walls, a dry riverbed road, dust plumes, arches and hoodoos, the helicopter thudding low between the walls, the sun blazing down the canyon throat. Each area of the frame intentionally composed, pristine and luxurious, with true foreground-to-background layering and immersive environmental depth. Architecture in fine premium materials with an immaculate finish.

Wardrobe: A fitted rust-and-bronze tactical jumpsuit with articulated drop-harness panels, a snug collar, bound forearms and a sashed waist over shin-guards — full-coverage, modest, stunt-rigger-coded.

Footwear: Bronze-laced tactical drop-boots with grippy soles and reinforced ankles — built for the impact of the drop.

Eye Colour: Warm amber-brown (consistent locked identity), blazing in the canyon light, fixed on the car below.

Accessories: A slim drop-harness with a quick-release, fingerless grip-gloves, dust-goggles on the brow, the thin titanium wrist cuff.

Hair: The same tight low braid, whipped by the rotor-wash, tail streaming.

Makeup: Matte sun-and-dust complexion, defined brow, cool eye, neutral lip; dust-film and sweat sheen permitted.

Movement (the one impossible move): Mid-drop, when a gust throws her off the car's roofline, the canyon dust does not let her fall — it gathers into a spinning vortex of golden sand that rises and cradles her, slowing her descent and setting her gently onto the speeding roof, then disperses down the canyon.

Concept Line: "She lets go of the sky — and the desert itself catches her onto the car."

Duration: ~24 seconds · 8 frames · 24fps · 180° shutter.

Mood: Vertiginous, daring, elemental, sublime.

Composition: Canyon-wall verticals, riverbed leading-lines, rotor-wash dust foregrounds, helicopter and car raked across thirds; intercut a hang-from-skid wide, a drop two-shot, an evasive mid-air twist hero beat, a sand-vortex catch reveal, a canyon-wall near-miss, and a hero stance on the roof. Premium foreground, midground and background layering with strong three-dimensional depth and clean visual balance (original framing preserved).

Grade: Rust-red and gold, canyon warmth, deep wall-shadow, incandescent gold for the sand-vortex magic, rich blacks, controlled bloom on the sun and dust. World-class editorial colour — rich and vivid yet clean, luminous mid-tones, deep-but-clean blacks, controlled saturation, nothing washed-out, natural skin protected.

Identity Safety: Aira rendered entirely from the locked reference sheet; adult, 25; no physical or ethnic enumeration; full-coverage wardrobe; stylized stunt — damage lands on rock, dust, and the cars only, no gore, no people harmed.

### 🎚️ Master Soundtrack & Sound-Design Brief — Audio Block B (outside the prompts)

Role: The continuous score and sound-design bed under the in-frame diegetic audio across all eight frames, binding them into one twenty-four-second piece. Genre & palette: Big desert-action-cinematic — driving taiko-and-tom percussion, a sub-heavy engine-tuned bass, a tense brass-and-string ostinato, a soaring vocal-and-string theme reserved for the sand-vortex catch, and rotor-and-engine-and-wind ambience. Tempo & key: 132 BPM, D minor, 4/4, locked so the drop and impacts land on the downbeat. Instrumentation by layer: sub and engine-thrum (the chase mass), taiko and toms (the drop and impacts), brass-and-string ostinato (the stunt tension), soaring vocal and strings (the sand-vortex magic), granular rotor-and-wind texture (atmosphere). Dynamic arc: Frame 1 sparse and thudding (rotor and drone only); Frames 2–3 taiko and bass enter with the hang and drop; Frame 4 a brass stab on the gust-hazard; Frame 5 a riser into the mid-air twist; Frame 6 the vocal-and-string theme blooms over a riser as the vortex catches her; Frame 7 a half-time breakdown for the canyon-wall near-miss; Frame 8 full triumphant tutti tailing to the opening rotor-thrum for a perfect loop. Hits & transitions: a taiko hit on the release (F2), a brass stab on the gust (F4), a riser into the twist (F5), a beat-dropout and 300ms suspended silence as the vortex gathers (F5→F6), a vocal-and-string swell through the catch (F6), an impact boom and brief near-silence at the canyon-wall near-miss (F7), a brass-and-bell stinger on the hero stance (F8). Mix: score about 6 dB under the rotor and engine-foley; sidechain the bed to the biggest hits; wide-stereo vocal and strings, mono-tight sub; tape saturation, vast canyon-reverb on the vocal. Loop point: the closing rotor-thrum matches bar one.

MASTER AUDIO: a 48-second custom cinematic score (8 clips × 6s) composed in self-resolving ~6-second phrases, so it can be trimmed to the reel’s assembled length at any phrase boundary without a hard cut — it always resolves, never stops dead or loops audibly. Built on deep trailer percussion, a driving synth bass and brass swells — premium, cinematic and emotionally catching, never generic stock or empty ambience. It opens immediately on a low brass-and-sub impact (no fade-in) so the mood lands within the first second. Emotional arc: an airy intimate open across the first beats, a rising shimmer of anticipation toward frame 5, the warm emotional peak on the signature move — Mid-drop, when a gust throws her off the car's roofline, the canyon dust does not let…, then a graceful resolve back toward the opening for an invisible loop. SPOKEN reel — the score opens space for the spoken line at its beat and ducks gently beneath the voice, then re-blooms after. Layered as one system — lead melody, warm sub for body, taut percussive tension as atmosphere, restrained percussion, and event impacts reserved for the major beats, each tuned to the score’s own key so every hit reads as the music itself expanding, never an added sound effect.

Wherever sky is visible, render it richly graded and cinematic — never empty, white or overexposed. Wherever water is visible, render it crystal-clear with deep sea-blue and turquoise gradients, clean caustics and sparkling reflections — never grey or murky (unless the concept explicitly specifies dark / ink / obsidian water, which stays deep, glassy and clean). Protect natural, true skin tone; never push saturation onto the face. Keep the image immaculately clean and premium with clear figure-to-ground separation so the hero reads first; honour the concept's intended dominant surface and full-frame coverage, and do NOT reveal unwanted structural edges, seams, side-walls or empty dead space that break the intended composition. Photoreal, award-winning editorial finish — no AI artifacts, plastic or waxy skin, dead eyes, malformed hands or unfinished backgrounds.

IDENTITY LOCK: Aira [paste identity-lock reference sheet here], continuity from Frame 5. Scene: the magic blooms — thrown wide of the car over the canyon, Aira does not fall: the canyon dust gathers into a spinning vortex of golden sand that rises and cradles her, slowing her descent and sweeping her gently back toward the speeding roof, the column of sand turning slow and luminous around her. WARDROBE: jumpsuit lit by the golden vortex, panels glowing. FOOTWEAR: drop-boots cradled in the rising sand, reaching for the roof. EYE COLOUR: amber-brown blazing, the sand-vortex mirrored in the iris. ACCESSORIES: harness streaming in the vortex, gloves, goggles, cuff. HAIR: braid lifting in the turning sand, edged in gold. MAKEUP: matte, golden glow across the cheek. HANDS & NAILS: gloved hands opening in wonder as the sand cradles her. EXPRESSION (into ): awe breaking into defiant thrill — lips parting, brows lifting at the rising sand, then a wild adrenaline grin, eyes bright and reckless. BODY POSTURE & WEIGHT: weight cradled and slowing in the vortex, chest lifting, shoulders opening with exhilaration. LIGHTING: KEY the self-luminous sand-vortex casting golden light onto her face; RIM glowing edge; FILL ambient bloom and canyon-glow. The beat: the magic blooms — thrown wide of the car over the canyon, Aira does not fall: the canyon dust gathers into a spinning vortex of golden sand that rises and cradles her, slowing her descent and sweeping her gently back toward the speeding roof, the column of sand turning slow and luminous around her. ORIENTATION LOCK — preserve this exact left-to-right composition; do not mirror, flip or invert the frame. LENS + DOF + BOKEH: 85mm-equivalent, f/2.8, spinning sand in luminous bokeh, sharp on her face. ATMOSPHERE: a turning suspended sand-vortex, drifting golden motes. COLOR GRADE: incandescent gold glow, deep canyon shadow for contrast, gentle bloom on the vortex. TEXTURE & MICRO-DETAIL: skin, fine grain-streaks in the turning vortex, glints riding the spiral, jumpsuit catching glow. NEGATIVE: no fall, no gore, no warped face, no garish clipping, no logos, no oversaturation beyond intended bloom, no sexualization, no enumeration, no text.

## Concept 17 — Horseback vs Motorcycle Plains Chase

Lane: Hybrid chase · a galloping horse outrunning and outmaneuvering a pursuing motorcycle across open plains.

Format: Seven-frame vertical action reel — one continuous chase as Aira rides a galloping horse across golden sunset grasslands, hunted by a dirt-motorcycle, rendered as seven seeded keyframes, roughly twenty-one seconds.

Hero Element: A powerful dark bay horse at full gallop — Aira rides low and fast, weaving the terrain. The horse is treated with care: spirited and sure-footed, never harmed, never spurred cruelly; all stylized damage lands on dust, fences, and the pursuing bike only.

Pursuer Element: A single rider on a dirt-motorcycle giving chase across the plains — an obstacle only, spun out into the grass, never wounded; the only damage is a dust-out and a dropped bike.

Setting: Vast golden grasslands at sunset — rolling hills, a lone oak, a split-rail fence line, a dry creek and a wooden bridge, tall grass rippling, the enormous low sun flooding the plain amber and the sky rose-gold. Every zone of the frame intentionally designed, immaculately clean and premium, with layered foreground, midground and background depth and rich environmental storytelling. Sky richly graded and cinematic with elegant clouds and premium light, never flat or empty. Greenery lush and healthy with premium landscaping.

Wardrobe: A fitted tan-and-oxblood riding coat with articulated panels, a high collar and a half-cape, bound forearms and riding breeches over tall boots — full-coverage, modest, equestrian-action-coded, streaming in the gallop.

Footwear: Tall oxblood riding boots with a defined heel and grippy sole, locked into the stirrups.

Eye Colour: Warm amber-brown (consistent locked identity), blazing in the sunset, fixed down the plain.

Accessories: Fingerless riding gloves, a slim crossbody satchel, a half-cape, the thin titanium wrist cuff; reins held sure (no harsh bit-pull shown).

Hair: The same tight low braid, streaming in the gallop-wind.

Makeup: Matte sun-warmed complexion, defined brow, cool eye, neutral lip; dust-film and sweat sheen permitted.

Movement (the one impossible move): As she crests a rise at full gallop, the great plume of golden dust thrown up by the horse does not scatter — it sweeps up into a vast phoenix of glowing dust that spreads its wings across the sunset behind her, beating once, then dissolves back into the grass.

Concept Line: "She rides the plain into the sun — and the dust rises behind her as a phoenix."

Duration: ~21 seconds · 7 frames · 24fps · 180° shutter.

Mood: Sweeping, free, windswept, sublime.

Composition: Horizon-wide leading-lines, lone-oak and fence verticals, tall-grass and dust foregrounds, horse and bike raked across thirds; intercut a gallop establishing wide, a chase two-shot, a fence-jump hero beat, a dust-phoenix reveal, and a hero rein-up. Rich layered foreground-to-background depth, elegant spatial hierarchy and clean visual flow (original framing and angle preserved).

Grade: Amber-and-oxblood, sunset gold, deep grass-shadow, incandescent gold for the dust-phoenix magic, rich blacks, controlled bloom on the sun and dust. World-class editorial colour — rich and vivid yet clean, luminous mid-tones, deep-but-clean blacks, controlled saturation, nothing washed-out, natural skin protected.

Identity Safety: Aira rendered entirely from the locked reference sheet; adult, 25; no physical or ethnic enumeration; full-coverage wardrobe; the horse treated humanely and never harmed; stylized chase — damage lands on dust, fences, and the bike only, no gore.

### 🎚️ Master Soundtrack & Sound-Design Brief — Audio Block B (outside the prompts)

Role: The continuous score and sound-design bed under the in-frame diegetic audio across all seven frames, binding them into one twenty-one-second piece. Genre & palette: Sweeping epic-Western-cinematic — galloping hand-percussion and toms, a warm cello-and-bass pulse, a soaring strings-and-whistle theme, a glowing choir-and-strings motif reserved for the dust-phoenix magic, and grass-wind-and-hoofbeat ambience. Tempo & key: 120 BPM matched to the gallop, G major turning to E minor under tension, 4/4. Instrumentation by layer: cello-and-bass pulse (the gallop's drive), toms and hand-percussion (the hoofbeats and jumps), strings-and-whistle (the soaring chase line), glowing choir-and-strings (the dust-phoenix magic), granular grass-wind texture (atmosphere). Dynamic arc: Frame 1 spare and warm (whistle and wind only); Frames 2–3 percussion and pulse enter with the launch and chase; Frame 4 a tension-stab on the hazard; Frame 5 a tom riser into the fence-jump; Frame 6 the choir-and-strings theme blooms over a riser as the phoenix rises; Frame 7 a warm resolving whistle phrase tailing to the opening theme for a perfect loop. Hits & transitions: a tom hit on the launch (F2), hoof-synced percussion through the chase (F3), a tension-stab on the hazard (F4), a tom riser into the jump (F5), a beat-dropout and 250ms suspended silence as the dust gathers (F5→F6), a choir-and-strings swell through the phoenix's wingbeat (F6), a soft whistle-and-string resolve on the rein-up (F7). Mix: score about 6 dB under the hoofbeats and wind; sidechain the bed to the biggest hoof-hits; wide-stereo strings and choir, mono-tight bass; tape warmth, wide-open plain-reverb on the whistle. Loop point: the closing theme matches bar one.

MASTER AUDIO: a 42-second custom cinematic score (7 clips × 6s) composed in self-resolving ~6-second phrases, so it can be trimmed to the reel’s assembled length at any phrase boundary without a hard cut — it always resolves, never stops dead or loops audibly. Built on deep trailer percussion, a driving synth bass and brass swells — premium, cinematic and emotionally catching, never generic stock or empty ambience. It opens immediately on a low brass-and-sub impact (no fade-in) so the mood lands within the first second. Emotional arc: an airy intimate open across the first beats, a rising shimmer of anticipation toward frame 5, the warm emotional peak on the signature move — As she crests a rise at full gallop, the great plume of golden dust thrown up…, then a graceful resolve back toward the opening for an invisible loop. SPOKEN reel — the score opens space for the spoken line at its beat and ducks gently beneath the voice, then re-blooms after. Layered as one system — lead melody, warm sub for body, taut percussive tension as atmosphere, restrained percussion, and event impacts reserved for the major beats, each tuned to the score’s own key so every hit reads as the music itself expanding, never an added sound effect.

### Frame 1 of 7 — IMAGE PROMPT
STRICT COLOUR & RENDER MANDATE (must be followed exactly; this governs render QUALITY only and must NOT change the composition, camera, lens, framing, wardrobe, action or identity specified above): render the colours already specified at their richest, freshest, cleanest and most premium — crisp, vivid and luminous with elegant, controlled saturation; never dull, faded, washed-out, muddy, grey or flat. Hold deep-but-clean blacks, luminous full mid-tones and beautiful highlight roll-off with high dynamic range and no blown highlights. Wherever sky is visible, render it richly graded and cinematic — never empty, white or overexposed. Wherever water is visible, render it crystal-clear with deep sea-blue and turquoise gradients, clean caustics and sparkling reflections — never grey or murky (unless the concept explicitly specifies dark / ink / obsidian water, which stays deep, glassy and clean). Protect natural, true skin tone; never push saturation onto the face. Keep the image immaculately clean and premium with clear figure-to-ground separation so the hero reads first; honour the concept's intended dominant surface and full-frame coverage, and do NOT reveal unwanted structural edges, seams, side-walls or empty dead space that break the intended composition. Photoreal, award-winning editorial finish — no AI artifacts, plastic or waxy skin, dead eyes, malformed hands or unfinished backgrounds.

IDENTITY LOCK: Aira [paste identity-lock reference sheet here]. Scene: she sits a dark bay horse at a standstill on a grassy rise in the flooding sunset, reins easy, glancing back at the distant dust-plume of the approaching motorcycle across the plain. WARDROBE: tan-and-oxblood riding coat, half-cape, collar up, breeze-stirred. FOOTWEAR: tall oxblood boots set in the stirrups. EYE COLOUR: warm amber-brown, golden sunset catchlight, gaze back at the dust-plume. ACCESSORIES: riding gloves, crossbody satchel, half-cape, titanium cuff, reins held easy. HAIR: tight low braid stirring in the breeze. MAKEUP: matte sun-warmed skin, defined brow, cool eye, neutral lip. HANDS & NAILS: short clean nails, gloved hands easy on the reins. EXPRESSION: cold-focus game face — steady level gaze back across the plain, lips in a strong neutral set, a slow controlled breath, calm intensity, faint jaw-tension. BODY POSTURE & WEIGHT: seated tall and balanced, weight settled in the saddle, shoulders square, living stillness — breath, the horse's flick of an ear, rippling grass alive. SPATIAL LOGIC: foreground rippling tall grass and the horse's mane; midground Aira mounted on the rise; background the distant motorcycle dust-plume, a lone oak, fence line, the enormous low sun. LIGHTING: KEY warm low sun from camera-back-left; FILL cool sky-bounce; RIM golden edge on braid, cape, and the horse; PRACTICAL sun-flare across the grass. FRAMING: ASPECT 9:16, medium-wide, low camera, grassland leading to the horizon, oak towering. SUBJECT FRAMING & POSITION: Framed as medium-wide, low camera, grassland leading to the horizon, oak towering, on a low hero angle looking up. Most of the figure is in frame with the environment around her. Her body is alive and committed to the motion, in dynamically angled to camera. Camera distance follows the shot size, holding the action sharp. The beat: she sits a dark bay horse at a standstill on a grassy rise in the flooding sunset, reins easy, glancing back at the distant dust-plume of the approaching motorcycle across the plain. Her pose reads as seated tall and balanced, weight settled in the saddle, shoulders square, living stillness — breath, the horse's flick of an ear, rippling grass alive — gaze pinned forward along the ride, fierce and steady; head low and committed in the line; both shoulders rolled forward over the mount; the RIGHT hand gripping for control while the LEFT hand braces and holds on; torso leaning into a committed riding crouch, core tight; waist set forward, hips locked to the mount; the RIGHT leg and LEFT leg clamped to the flanks with knees gripping; feet set and braced, weight committed forward over the mount; hair streaming back off the speed. ORIENTATION LOCK — preserve this exact left-to-right composition; do not mirror, flip or invert the frame. LENS + DOF + BOKEH: 35mm-equivalent, f/2.8, sharp on her and the horse, plain to bokeh. ATMOSPHERE: rippling grass, golden dust-haze, sun-flare. COLOR GRADE: amber-and-oxblood, sunset gold, deep grass-shadow, controlled bloom. TEXTURE & MICRO-DETAIL: pores and sun-dust, coat-weave and cape, glove-grip, the horse's coat-sheen and mane detail. NEGATIVE: no extra people up close, no facial blur, no distorted hands, no harm to the horse, no gore, no logos or text, no warped anatomy, no oversaturation, no sexualization, no physical or ethnic enumeration, no watermark.

### Frame 1 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT
STRICT COLOUR & RENDER MANDATE (must be followed exactly; this governs render QUALITY only and must NOT change the composition, camera, lens, framing, wardrobe, action or identity specified above): render the colours already specified at their richest, freshest, cleanest and most premium — crisp, vivid and luminous with elegant, controlled saturation; never dull, faded, washed-out, muddy, grey or flat. Hold deep-but-clean blacks, luminous full mid-tones and beautiful highlight roll-off with high dynamic range and no blown highlights. Wherever sky is visible, render it richly graded and cinematic — never empty, white or overexposed. Wherever water is visible, render it crystal-clear with deep sea-blue and turquoise gradients, clean caustics and sparkling reflections — never grey or murky (unless the concept explicitly specifies dark / ink / obsidian water, which stays deep, glassy and clean). Protect natural, true skin tone; never push saturation onto the face. Keep the image immaculately clean and premium with clear figure-to-ground separation so the hero reads first; honour the concept's intended dominant surface and full-frame coverage, and do NOT reveal unwanted structural edges, seams, side-walls or empty dead space that break the intended composition. Photoreal, award-winning editorial finish — no AI artifacts, plastic or waxy skin, dead eyes, malformed hands or unfinished backgrounds.

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: Medium-wide hero establishing shot, low angle. CAMERA MOVEMENT: one move only — a slow 1.2x push-in toward Aira on the rise. LENS: 35mm-equivalent, f/2.8. PHYSICS: tall grass ripples in waves of wind; the horse shifts and tosses its head; the distant dust-plume drifts closer; the cape stirs; no chase yet. SUBJECT + OBJECT ACTION (timecoded): 0.0–2.0s Aira glances back and ranges the approaching dust-plume, reins easy (grass rippling, horse shifting); 2.0–4.0s she gathers the reins and turns the horse forward down the rise, gaze hardening; 4.0–6.0s she settles into the saddle, the only motion rippling grass and the horse's breath. EXPRESSION TRANSITION: throughout — neutral set tightening to a faint eye-narrow by 5.0s as she commits; no smile. LIGHTING: warm low-sun key, sky fill, sun-flare practicals. ENVIRONMENT: sunset grassland, distant motorcycle, lone oak. AUDIO (Block A, in-frame diegetic): a warm grass-wind bed, the horse's breath and a tail-swish, a distant motorcycle-buzz, a saddle-creak at 3.2s; score whistle and wind only underneath. DURATION: 6.0s. FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180°, natural blur on grass and cape. LIP-SYNC: none, lips set. NEGATIVE: no cuts, no second camera move, no chase yet, no harm to the horse, no extra people, no warping, no text.
