Hold deep-but-clean blacks, luminous full mid-tones and beautiful highlight roll-off with high dynamic range and no blown highlights. Wherever sky is visible, render it richly graded and cinematic — never empty, white or overexposed. Wherever water is visible, render it crystal-clear with deep sea-blue and turquoise gradients, clean caustics and sparkling reflections — never grey or murky (unless the concept explicitly specifies dark / ink / obsidian water, which stays deep, glassy and clean). Protect natural, true skin tone; never push saturation onto the face. Keep the image immaculately clean and premium with clear figure-to-ground separation so the hero reads first; honour the concept's intended dominant surface and full-frame coverage, and do NOT reveal unwanted structural edges, seams, side-walls or empty dead space that break the intended composition. Photoreal, award-winning editorial finish — no AI artifacts, plastic or waxy skin, dead eyes, malformed hands or unfinished backgrounds.

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: Heroic low-angle finale shot. CAMERA MOVEMENT: one move only — a slow push-in and slight rise settling on Aira and the horse. LENS: 35mm-equivalent, f/4. PHYSICS: the horse eases from gallop to a soft halt on the crest; the last dust-motes drift up and fade; Aira settles tall in the saddle; the plain glows under the low sun. SUBJECT + OBJECT ACTION (timecoded): 0.0–2.0s Aira eases the horse to a soft halt on the crest, the spun-out motorcycle stalled far back (dust drifting up, plain glowing); 2.0–4.0s she settles tall in the saddle and rests a hand on the horse's neck, braid loosening; 4.0–6.0s she exhales a long breath and a brief fierce smile breaks as the last dust-motes fade gold around them. EXPRESSION TRANSITION: into into — hard focus melts to a heaving-chest release at 2.8s, resolving into eased-brow triumph and a brief fierce smile by 5.2s. LIGHTING: warm low-sun key, golden rim, fading dust practicals. ENVIRONMENT: glowing plain, stalled motorcycle, low sun. AUDIO (Block A): the hoofbeats settling to a soft halt, the horse's heavy breath and a snort, warm grass-wind, a long exhale at 2.8s, a final faint shimmer as the dust fades; score lands a soft whistle-and-string resolve then tails to the opening theme for the loop. DURATION: 6.0s. FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180°, gentle blur on the rise, crisp on the face. LIP-SYNC: none; an exhale and a closed-lip smile. NEGATIVE: no harm to the horse, no gore, no cut, no second move, no extra people up close, no warped geometry, no text or watermark.

---

## Concept 18 — Megacity Parkour Foot-Chase

Lane: Foot chase + unarmed combat · a rooftop parkour pursuit through a neon megacity that turns and becomes a fight.

Format: Seven-frame vertical action reel — one continuous night parkour chase across neon rooftops that resolves into a hand-to-hand stand, rendered as seven seeded keyframes, roughly twenty-one seconds.

Hero Element: No weapon — Aira runs, vaults, and leaps the rooftops with mind-blowing free-running, then turns on her pursuer with a signature spinning kick. Realistic wear only — scuffed palms, neon-wet jacket — no blood; combat contact lands on the pursuer's guard and gear only.

Pursuer Element: A single masked tracker chasing her across the rooftops — no weapons, taking the impact on guard and gear and being downed onto an AC unit, never wounded.

Setting: A towering neon megacity at night in light rain — mirrored glass skyscrapers, holographic billboards, rooftop gardens, AC units and antennae, a chasm of glowing streets far below, rain misting through magenta-and-cyan light. Every zone of the frame intentionally designed, immaculately clean and premium, with layered foreground, midground and background depth and rich environmental storytelling. Water rendered crystal-clear in rich sea-blue and turquoise gradients, with beautiful caustics and clean sparkling reflections. Greenery lush and healthy with premium landscaping.

Wardrobe: A fitted charcoal techwear jacket with reflective seams and articulated panels, a high collar, bound forearms and tapered cargo trousers over grip-soled trainers — full-coverage, modest, urban-runner-coded, neon-wet.

Footwear: Black-and-cyan grip-soled parkour trainers, low-profile and flexible — essential for the vaults and the spin.

Eye Colour: Warm amber-brown (consistent locked identity), catching magenta-and-cyan neon, fixed ahead.

Accessories: A slim crossbody runner-pack, fingerless grip-gloves, a thin neck-gaiter (pulled up over nose and mouth from Frame 4), the thin titanium wrist cuff.

Hair: The same tight low braid, rain-damp, tail snapping with each leap.

Makeup: Matte neon-lit complexion, defined brow, cool eye, neutral lip; rain-sheen permitted.

Movement (the one impossible move): At the apex of her signature spinning kick, the mirrored-glass facade behind her does not just reflect — the reflections peel free into a vast corridor of floating mirror-panes that fan out around her, each holding a glowing echo of her kick, then collapse back into the glass.

Concept Line: "She runs the skyline — and the whole city turns to mirrors around her strike."

Duration: ~21 seconds · 7 frames · 24fps · 180° shutter.

Mood: Electric, vertiginous, sleek, sublime.

Composition: Skyscraper verticals, rooftop leading-lines, neon-rain foregrounds, runner and pursuer raked across thirds; intercut a rooftop sprint wide, a vault two-shot, a gap-leap hero beat, a 360-kick beat, a mirror-corridor reveal, and a hero stance. Rich layered foreground-to-background depth, elegant spatial hierarchy and clean visual flow (original framing and angle preserved).

Grade: Magenta-and-cyan neon, rain-wet blacks, deep city-chasm shadow, prismatic silver-and-cyan for the mirror magic, rich blacks, controlled bloom on neon and rain. World-class editorial colour — rich and vivid yet clean, luminous mid-tones, deep-but-clean blacks, controlled saturation, nothing washed-out, natural skin protected.

Identity Safety: Aira rendered entirely from the locked reference sheet; adult, 25; no physical or ethnic enumeration; full-coverage wardrobe; stylized parkour and unarmed combat — impacts land on guard and gear only, no gore, no weapons, no fall.

### 🎚️ Master Soundtrack & Sound-Design Brief — Audio Block B (outside the prompts)

Role: The continuous score and sound-design bed under the in-frame diegetic audio across all seven frames, binding them into one twenty-one-second piece. Genre & palette: Dark synthwave-meets-orchestral — a driving arpeggiated synth-bass, punchy electronic drums, a tense string-and-brass ostinato, a shimmering glass-bell-and-choir theme reserved for the mirror-corridor magic, and rain-and-city ambience. Tempo & key: 138 BPM, B minor, 4/4, locked so footfalls and impacts land on the beat. Instrumentation by layer: synth-sub and arpeggio (the run's drive), punchy kick and snare (the footfalls and impacts), string-and-brass ostinato (the chase tension), shimmering glass-bells and wordless choir (the mirror magic), granular rain-and-city texture (atmosphere). Dynamic arc: Frame 1 sparse and pulsing (arpeggio and rain only); Frames 2–3 drums and bass enter with the sprint and vaults; Frame 4 a brass stab on the gap-hazard; Frame 5 a riser into the 360 kick with one big hit on contact; Frame 6 the glass-bell-and-choir theme blooms over a riser as the mirrors peel; Frame 7 a resolving synth-and-bell phrase tailing to the opening arpeggio for a perfect loop. Hits & transitions: a kick-drop on the first vault (F2), synced footfall-percussion through the run (F3), a brass stab on the gap (F4), a riser into the spin (F5), one big impact-hit and a 250ms beat-dropout on the kick's contact (F5), a glass-bell swell as the mirrors peel (F6), a soft synth-and-bell resolve on the hero stance (F7). Mix: score about 6 dB under the footfalls and combat-impacts; sidechain the bed to the biggest hits; wide-stereo bells and choir, mono-tight sub; tape saturation, lush plate-reverb on the bells. Loop point: the closing arpeggio matches bar one.

MASTER AUDIO: a 42-second custom cinematic score (7 clips × 6s) composed in self-resolving ~6-second phrases, so it can be trimmed to the reel’s assembled length at any phrase boundary without a hard cut — it always resolves, never stops dead or loops audibly. Built on deep trailer percussion, a driving synth bass and brass swells — premium, cinematic and emotionally catching, never generic stock or empty ambience. It opens immediately on a low brass-and-sub impact (no fade-in) so the mood lands within the first second. Emotional arc: an airy intimate open across the first beats, a rising shimmer of anticipation toward frame 5, the warm emotional peak on the signature move — At the apex of her signature spinning kick, the mirrored-glass facade behind her does not just…, then a graceful resolve back toward the opening for an invisible loop. SPOKEN reel — the score opens space for the spoken line at its beat and ducks gently beneath the voice, then re-blooms after. Layered as one system — lead melody, warm sub for body, taut percussive tension as atmosphere, restrained percussion, and event impacts reserved for the major beats, each tuned to the score’s own key so every hit reads as the music itself expanding, never an added sound effect.

ORIENTATION LOCK — preserve this exact left-to-right composition; do not mirror, flip or invert the frame. LENS + DOF + BOKEH: 35mm-equivalent, f/2.8, sharp on her, tracker and city to neon-bokeh. ATMOSPHERE: misting rain, neon-haze, holographic flicker. COLOR GRADE: magenta-and-cyan neon, rain-wet blacks, deep chasm-shadow, controlled bloom. TEXTURE & MICRO-DETAIL: pores and rain-beads, techwear weave and reflective seams, gloved knuckles, wet-gravel and rooftop-duct detail. NEGATIVE: no extra people beyond the tracker, no facial blur, no distorted hands, no weapons, no logos or text, no warped city geometry, no oversaturation, no sexualization, no physical or ethnic enumeration, no watermark.

TEXTURE & MICRO-DETAIL: skin, crisp glass-edge and reflection detail in each pane, neon refractions, rain on the jacket catching glow. NEGATIVE: no extra people beyond the reflected tracker, no weapons, no warped face, no garish clipping, no logos, no oversaturation beyond intended bloom, no sexualization, no enumeration, no text.

### Frame 6 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT
STRICT COLOUR & RENDER MANDATE (must be followed exactly; this governs render QUALITY only and must NOT change the composition, camera, lens, framing, wardrobe, action or identity specified above): render the colours already specified at their richest, freshest, cleanest and most premium — crisp, vivid and luminous with elegant, controlled saturation; never dull, faded, washed-out, muddy, grey or flat. Hold deep-but-clean blacks, luminous full mid-tones and beautiful highlight roll-off with high dynamic range and no blown highlights. Wherever sky is visible, render it richly graded and cinematic — never empty, white or overexposed. Wherever water is visible, render it crystal-clear with deep sea-blue and turquoise gradients, clean caustics and sparkling reflections — never grey or murky (unless the concept explicitly specifies dark / ink / obsidian water, which stays deep, glassy and clean). Protect natural, true skin tone; never push saturation onto the face. Keep the image immaculately clean and premium with clear figure-to-ground separation so the hero reads first; honour the concept's intended dominant surface and full-frame coverage, and do NOT reveal unwanted structural edges, seams, side-walls or empty dead space that break the intended composition. Photoreal, award-winning editorial finish — no AI artifacts, plastic or waxy skin, dead eyes, malformed hands or unfinished backgrounds.

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: Hero three-quarter reveal shot. CAMERA MOVEMENT: one move only — a slow rise-and-settle (crane up) revealing the mirror-corridor. LENS: 85mm-equivalent, f/2.8. PHYSICS: the mirrored-glass facade peels free into floating mirror-panes that fan into a vast corridor around her, each holding a glowing echo of her kick, holding their formation, then collapse back into the glass; light refracts between the panes. SUBJECT + OBJECT ACTION (timecoded): 0.0–2.0s as Aira sets from the kick, the mirrored facade behind her shivers and begins to peel free into floating panes (glass lifting away); 2.0–4.0s the panes fan out into a vast corridor around her, each holding a glowing neon echo of her kick, the tracker reflected a hundredfold, her gaiter lowering; 4.0–6.0s the corridor collapses gracefully back into the facade, glints streaming off. EXPRESSION TRANSITION: into into — strained effort releases into widened-eye awe at 2.4s as the mirrors peel, blooming into a fierce grin by 4.8s. LIGHTING: self-luminous mirror-corridor up-lighting her face, glowing rim. ENVIRONMENT: the city turned to a corridor of mirrors around her. AUDIO (Block A): the city-hum dipping as the glass peels, a shimmering crystalline ring and a deep harmonic swell from 2.0s, a soft fierce exhale at 4.8s; score blooms the glass-bell-and-choir theme over a riser. DURATION: 6.0s. FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180°, silky blur on the rise, crisp floating mirrors. LIP-SYNC: none; a breath of wonder. NEGATIVE: no cut, no second move, no cartoonish glow, no weapons, no warped face, no text.

## Concept 19 — Wingsuit Canyon Sky-Chase

Lane: Air stunt · a wingsuit flight threading a mountain canyon, racing a pursuing flyer.

Format: Eight-frame vertical action reel — one continuous wingsuit flight diving and threading a vast mountain canyon at dawn, rendered as eight seeded keyframes, roughly twenty-four seconds.

Hero Element: A sleek charcoal-and-aurora wingsuit — Aira flies at full speed, threading rock gaps and skimming ridgelines with mind-blowing precision. Realistic flight physics and stylized non-gory wear only — wind-scour, a frost-rimed visor — no blood.

Pursuer Element: A single rival wingsuit-flyer racing her through the canyon — an obstacle and a rival only, peeling off safely, never harmed; the only damage is to powder-snow cornices and spray.

Setting: A colossal alpine canyon at dawn — sheer granite walls, snow-laced ridges, a glacial river thread far below, drifting clouds caught in the gorge, the first sun firing the peaks rose-gold while the depths stay blue-shadowed. Every zone of the frame intentionally designed, immaculately clean and premium, with layered foreground, midground and background depth and rich environmental storytelling. Water kept deep, clean and glassy — premium and clear, never muddy or grey. Sky richly graded and cinematic with elegant clouds and premium light, never flat or empty.

Wardrobe: A fitted charcoal-and-aurora wingsuit with aurora-iridescent wing-membranes, articulated arm and leg foils, a snug high collar and integrated harness over the suit — full-coverage, modest, aerial-athlete-coded.

Footwear: Aero jump-boots with rigid soles and integrated leg-wing anchors, locked into the suit's foils.

Eye Colour: Warm amber-brown (consistent locked identity), blazing behind a clear visor, fixed down the canyon.

Accessories: A clear aero-visor (down throughout flight), integrated harness, fingerless wing-grip gloves, a slim chute-pack, the thin titanium wrist cuff.

Hair: The same tight low braid, tucked into the collar, the tail snapping free in the slipstream.

Makeup: Matte wind-bitten complexion, defined brow, cool eye, neutral lip; visor-frost and cold-flush permitted.

Movement (the one impossible move): As she threads a cloud-filled gap at full speed, her wingtips do not just trail vapor — they peel two vast glowing ribbons of aurora-light through the cloud that hang in the air mapping her exact flight-path, arcing through the canyon behind her, then fade into the dawn.

Concept Line: "She falls through the mountains — and paints the canyon with light."

Duration: ~24 seconds · 8 frames · 24fps · 180° shutter.

Mood: Soaring, vertiginous, crystalline, sublime.

Composition: Canyon-wall verticals, ridgeline leading-lines, cloud-wisp foregrounds, the two flyers raked across thirds; intercut a cliff-exit wide, a dive two-shot, an evasive gap-thread hero beat, an aurora-trail reveal, a rock-gap near-miss, and a hero flare-out landing. Premium foreground, midground and background layering with strong three-dimensional depth and clean visual balance (original framing preserved).

Grade: Charcoal-and-aurora, dawn rose-gold on the peaks, blue-shadow depths, iridescent aurora-green-and-violet for the light-trail magic, rich blacks, controlled bloom on the sun and clouds. Premium editorial grade — rich clean colour, luminous mid-tones, deep-but-clean blacks, controlled elegant saturation, no faded or muddy tones, natural true skin preserved.

Identity Safety: Aira rendered entirely from the locked reference sheet; adult, 25; no physical or ethnic enumeration; full-coverage wardrobe; stylized aerial stunt — damage lands on snow and spray only, no gore, no people harmed.

### 🎚️ Master Soundtrack & Sound-Design Brief — Audio Block B (outside the prompts)

Role: The continuous score and sound-design bed under the in-frame diegetic audio across all eight frames, binding them into one twenty-four-second piece. Genre & palette: Soaring epic-cinematic — a pulsing low-string-and-sub drive, cinematic taiko swells, a tense brass ostinato, a vast soaring choir-and-strings theme reserved for the aurora-trail magic, and wind-rush ambience. Tempo & key: 126 BPM, E major lifting to C-sharp minor under tension, 4/4, locked so the dives and gap-threads land on the accent. Instrumentation by layer: sub and low-string drive (the dive's mass), taiko swells (the launch and threads), brass ostinato (the chase tension), vast choir-and-strings (the aurora-trail magic), granular wind-rush texture (atmosphere). Dynamic arc: Frame 1 spare and airy (high-strings and wind only); Frames 2–3 sub-drive and taiko enter with the exit and dive; Frame 4 a brass stab on the wall-hazard; Frame 5 a riser into the gap-thread; Frame 6 the choir-and-strings theme blooms over a riser as the aurora-trails peel; Frame 7 a half-time breakdown for the rock-gap near-miss; Frame 8 a warm resolving choir phrase tailing to the opening high-strings for a perfect loop. Hits & transitions: a taiko swell on the cliff-exit (F2), a brass stab on the wall-hazard (F4), a riser into the gap-thread (F5), a beat-dropout and 300ms suspended silence as the trails peel (F5→F6), a choir-and-strings swell through the aurora-trails (F6), an impact boom and brief near-silence at the rock-gap near-miss (F7), a soft choir-and-bell resolve on the flare-out (F8). Mix: score about 6 dB under the wind-rush and suit-flap; sidechain the bed to the biggest swells; wide-stereo choir and strings, mono-tight sub; tape warmth, vast canyon-reverb on the choir. Loop point: the closing high-strings match bar one.

MASTER AUDIO: a 48-second custom cinematic score (8 clips × 6s) composed in self-resolving ~6-second phrases, so it can be trimmed to the reel’s assembled length at any phrase boundary without a hard cut — it always resolves, never stops dead or loops audibly. Built on a glassy harp, an airy choir pad and soft weightless textures — premium, cinematic and emotionally catching, never generic stock or empty ambience. It opens immediately on a shimmering harp sweep over a choir-pad bloom (no fade-in) so the mood lands within the first second. Emotional arc: an airy intimate open across the first beats, a rising shimmer of anticipation toward frame 5, the warm emotional peak on the signature move — As she threads a cloud-filled gap at full speed, her wingtips do not just trail vapor, then a graceful resolve back toward the opening for an invisible loop. SPOKEN reel — the score opens space for the spoken line at its beat and ducks gently beneath the voice, then re-blooms after. Layered as one system — lead melody, warm sub for body, weightless choir shimmer as atmosphere, restrained percussion, and event impacts reserved for the major beats, each tuned to the score’s own key so every hit reads as the music itself expanding, never an added sound effect.

SUBJECT + OBJECT ACTION (timecoded): 0.0–2.0s Aira bursts from the gorge into the glowing valley and flares her wings up, the rival arcing away in acknowledgment (chute streaming open, trails fading); 2.0–4.0s she soars up into the flare under the open chute, gloved hand rising, braid streaming; 4.0–6.0s she exhales a long breath and a brief fierce smile breaks as the last aurora-trails fade behind her. EXPRESSION TRANSITION: into into — hard focus melts to a heaving-chest release at 2.8s, resolving into eased-brow triumph and a brief fierce smile by 5.2s. LIGHTING: warm dawn-valley key, rose-gold rim, fading aurora practicals. ENVIRONMENT: open gold valley, arcing-away rival, gorge behind. AUDIO (Block A): the gorge-wind opening into wide calm valley-air, a chute-crack and canopy-flap at 1.2s, a long exhale at 2.8s, a final faint shimmer as the trails fade; score lands a soft choir-and-bell resolve then tails to the opening high-strings for the loop. DURATION: 6.0s. FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180°, gentle blur on the rise, crisp on the face. LIP-SYNC: none; an exhale and a closed-lip smile. NEGATIVE: no gore, no crash, no cut, no second move, no extra people up close, no warped geometry, no text or watermark.

---

## Concept 20 — Phoenix Convoy Finale

Lane: Convoy + unarmed combat · a rooftop fight across a speeding desert convoy that ends the whole set in fire and rebirth.

Format: Eight-frame vertical action reel — one continuous unarmed fight as Aira leaps and battles across the roofs of a speeding armored convoy at sunset, rendered as eight seeded keyframes, roughly twenty-four seconds — the grand finale of the set.

Hero Element: No weapon — Aira runs, leaps, and fights roof-to-roof along a moving convoy with mind-blowing choreography, finishing with a signature spinning kick. Realistic wear only — scuffed knuckles, dust-caked jacket — no blood; combat contact lands on guards and gear only.

Pursuer Element: Two masked convoy-guards on the vehicle roofs — no weapons drawn into killing use; taking impacts on guard and gear and being downed onto the rolling roofs, never wounded; the only destruction is to the vehicles themselves.

Setting: A long desert highway at blazing sunset — a convoy of three armored trucks thundering across red-rock badlands, dust-plumes boiling behind, mesas and a vast crimson sky, the lowering sun firing the dust and steel. Each area of the frame intentionally composed, pristine and luxurious, with true foreground-to-background layering and immersive environmental depth. Sky richly graded and cinematic with elegant clouds and premium light, never flat or empty.

Wardrobe: A fitted oxblood-and-charcoal combat jacket with armored shoulders and articulated panels, a high collar, bound forearms and tactical trousers over grip-soled boots — full-coverage, modest, action-coded, dust-caked and streaming.

Footwear: Oxblood grip-soled tactical boots with a defined heel — essential traction on the rolling truck-roofs and for the spin.

Eye Colour: Warm amber-brown (consistent locked identity), blazing in the sunset, fixed down the convoy.

Accessories: Fingerless tactical gloves, a slim crossbody rig, a thin neck-scarf (pulled up against the dust from Frame 4), the thin titanium wrist cuff.

Hair: The same tight low braid, dust-laced, tail snapping with each leap.

Makeup: Matte sun-and-dust complexion, defined brow, cool eye, neutral lip; dust-film and sweat sheen permitted.

Movement (the one impossible move): As her finishing spinning kick lands and a truck bursts into a shower of sparks and embers, the sparks do not scatter — they sweep up into a vast phoenix of fire and embers that spreads its wings over the whole convoy and beats once, the perfect closing image of the entire set, then settles into drifting embers.

Concept Line: "She ends the road in fire — and from the wreck a phoenix rises."

Duration: ~24 seconds · 8 frames · 24fps · 180° shutter.

Mood: Epic, blazing, triumphant, sublime — the finale.

Composition: Convoy leading-lines, mesa and sun verticals, dust-plume and spark foregrounds, Aira and guards raked across thirds; intercut a convoy establishing wide, a roof-leap two-shot, a fight clash beat, a 360-kick beat, a spark-phoenix reveal, a roof-gap near-miss, and a hero stance against the sun. Premium foreground, midground and background layering with strong three-dimensional depth and clean visual balance (original framing preserved).

Grade: Oxblood-and-charcoal, sunset crimson-and-gold, deep dust-shadow, incandescent fire-orange-and-gold for the phoenix magic, rich blacks, controlled bloom on the sun, dust, and sparks. World-class editorial colour — rich and vivid yet clean, luminous mid-tones, deep-but-clean blacks, controlled saturation, nothing washed-out, natural skin protected.

Identity Safety: Aira rendered entirely from the locked reference sheet; adult, 25; no physical or ethnic enumeration; full-coverage wardrobe; stylized convoy combat — destruction lands on the vehicles, dust, and gear only, impacts on guards land on guard and gear, no gore, no weapons used to wound, no fall.

### 🎚️ Master Soundtrack & Sound-Design Brief — Audio Block B (outside the prompts)

Role: The continuous score and sound-design bed under the in-frame diegetic audio across all eight frames, binding them into one twenty-four-second piece — and the closing theme of the whole set. Genre & palette: Epic hybrid-orchestral-action — a thunderous taiko-and-sub engine-pulse, driving low brass, a tense string ostinato, a vast triumphant choir-brass-and-strings theme reserved for the spark-phoenix magic, and engine-and-dust ambience. Tempo & key: 132 BPM, D minor rising to D major for the finale, 4/4, locked so footfalls and impacts land on the beat. Instrumentation by layer: sub-and-taiko engine-pulse (the convoy's mass), driving low brass (the chase drive), string ostinato (the fight tension), vast choir-brass-and-strings (the phoenix magic and finale), granular engine-and-dust texture (atmosphere). Dynamic arc: Frame 1 ominous and pulsing (low taiko and engine only); Frames 2–3 brass and strings enter with the leap and clash; Frame 4 a brass stab on the gap-hazard; Frame 5 a riser into the 360 kick with one huge hit on contact; Frame 6 the full choir-brass-and-strings theme blooms over a riser as the phoenix rises — the emotional peak of the whole set; Frame 7 a half-time breakdown for the roof-gap near-miss; Frame 8 a vast resolving major-key choir-and-brass swell that tails to the opening taiko for a perfect loop and a set-closing button. Hits & transitions: a taiko hit on the first roof-leap (F2), synced footfall-and-impact percussion through the clash (F3), a brass stab on the gap (F4), a riser into the spin (F5), one huge impact-hit and a 300ms beat-dropout on the kick's contact (F5), a full choir-brass swell through the phoenix's wingbeat (F6), an impact boom and brief near-silence at the roof-gap near-miss (F7), a triumphant choir-and-brass resolve on the hero stance (F8). Mix: score about 5 dB under the engines, footfalls, and impacts at peak; sidechain the bed to the biggest hits; wide-stereo choir, brass, and strings, mono-tight sub; tape saturation, vast desert-reverb on the choir. Loop point: the closing taiko matches bar one.

MASTER AUDIO: a 48-second custom cinematic score (8 clips × 6s) composed in self-resolving ~6-second phrases, so it can be trimmed to the reel’s assembled length at any phrase boundary without a hard cut — it always resolves, never stops dead or loops audibly. Built on deep trailer percussion, a driving synth bass and brass swells — premium, cinematic and emotionally catching, never generic stock or empty ambience. It opens immediately on a low brass-and-sub impact (no fade-in) so the mood lands within the first second. Emotional arc: an airy intimate open across the first beats, a rising shimmer of anticipation toward frame 5, the warm emotional peak on the signature move — As her finishing spinning kick lands and a truck bursts into a shower of sparks and…, then a graceful resolve back toward the opening for an invisible loop. SPOKEN reel — the score opens space for the spoken line at its beat and ducks gently beneath the voice, then re-blooms after. Layered as one system — lead melody, warm sub for body, taut percussive tension as atmosphere, restrained percussion, and event impacts reserved for the major beats, each tuned to the score’s own key so every hit reads as the music itself expanding, never an added sound effect.

### Frame 1 of 8 — IMAGE PROMPT
STRICT COLOUR & RENDER MANDATE (must be followed exactly; this governs render QUALITY only and must NOT change the composition, camera, lens, framing, wardrobe, action or identity specified above): render the colours already specified at their richest, freshest, cleanest and most premium — crisp, vivid and luminous with elegant, controlled saturation; never dull, faded, washed-out, muddy, grey or flat. Hold deep-but-clean blacks, luminous full mid-tones and beautiful highlight roll-off with high dynamic range and no blown highlights. Wherever sky is visible, render it richly graded and cinematic — never empty, white or overexposed. Wherever water is visible, render it crystal-clear with deep sea-blue and turquoise gradients, clean caustics and sparkling reflections — never grey or murky (unless the concept explicitly specifies dark / ink / obsidian water, which stays deep, glassy and clean). Protect natural, true skin tone; never push saturation onto the face. Keep the image immaculately clean and premium with clear figure-to-ground separation so the hero reads first; honour the concept's intended dominant surface and full-frame coverage, and do NOT reveal unwanted structural edges, seams, side-walls or empty dead space that break the intended composition. Photoreal, award-winning editorial finish — no AI artifacts, plastic or waxy skin, dead eyes, malformed hands or unfinished backgrounds.

IDENTITY LOCK: Aira [paste identity-lock reference sheet here]. Scene: she crouches on the roof of the rearmost speeding armored truck at sunset, gloved fingers braced to the hot steel, glancing forward along the convoy at the two masked guards rising on the trucks ahead, dust boiling behind. WARDROBE: oxblood-and-charcoal combat jacket, armored shoulders, collar up, dust-caked. FOOTWEAR: grip-soled tactical boots braced on the rolling roof. EYE COLOUR: warm amber-brown, sunset catchlight, gaze forward along the convoy. ACCESSORIES: crossbody rig, tactical gloves, neck-scarf down, titanium cuff. HAIR: tight low braid, dust-laced, tail snapping in the slipstream. MAKEUP: matte sun-and-dust skin, defined brow, cool eye, neutral lip. HANDS & NAILS: short clean nails, gloved fingers braced to the truck-roof. EXPRESSION: cold-focus game face — steady level gaze down the convoy, lips in a strong neutral set, a slow controlled breath, calm intensity, faint jaw-tension. BODY POSTURE & WEIGHT: coiled low on the rolling roof, weight braced against the speed, shoulders set, living stillness — breath, whipping braid, boiling dust alive. SPATIAL LOGIC: foreground the hot truck-roof and her braced hand; midground Aira crouched to move; background the two guards rising on the trucks ahead, the convoy, mesas, the boiling dust-plume, the lowering sun. LIGHTING: KEY warm low sun from camera-front; FILL warm dust-bounce; RIM gold edge on braid and shoulder; PRACTICAL sun-flare through the dust. FRAMING: ASPECT 9:16, medium-wide, low camera on the roof, convoy leading to the horizon, mesas towering. SUBJECT FRAMING & POSITION: Framed as medium-wide, low camera on the roof, convoy leading to the horizon, mesas towering, on a low hero angle looking up. Most of the figure is in frame with the environment around her. Her body is alive and committed to the motion, in dynamically angled to camera. Camera distance follows the shot size, holding the action sharp. The beat: she crouches on the roof of the rearmost speeding armored truck at sunset, gloved fingers braced to the hot steel, glancing forward along the convoy at the two masked guards rising on the trucks ahead, dust boiling behind. Her pose reads as coiled low on the rolling roof, weight braced against the speed, shoulders set, living stillness — breath, whipping braid, boiling dust alive — gaze level and reading the scene with cold focus; head steady, turned to the action; both shoulders settled and squared, alive with breath; the RIGHT arm poised and ready with the hand relaxed but set; the LEFT arm easy and counter-balanced at her side; torso upright and composed in living stillness; waist long with the hips squared, weight carried on the back foot; the RIGHT leg bearing the load with a soft knee while the LEFT braces with one heel just lifted; both feet planted and grounded, weight back and ready to spring; hair alive in the wind, sealed and controlled. ORIENTATION LOCK — preserve this exact left-to-right composition; do not mirror, flip or invert the frame. LENS + DOF + BOKEH: 35mm-equivalent, f/2.8, sharp on her, convoy and guards to dust-bokeh. ATMOSPHERE: boiling dust, heat-shimmer, sun-flare. COLOR GRADE: oxblood-and-charcoal, sunset crimson-and-gold, deep dust-shadow, controlled bloom. TEXTURE & MICRO-DETAIL: pores and dust-film, jacket-weave and armored shoulder, gloved knuckles, hot-steel roof and rivet detail. NEGATIVE: no extra people beyond the two guards, no facial blur, no distorted hands, no weapons in killing use, no logos or text, no warped vehicle geometry, no oversaturation, no sexualization, no physical or ethnic enumeration, no watermark.

Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

EXPRESSION TRANSITION: throughout — neutral set tightening to a faint eye-narrow by 5.0s as she commits; no smile. LIGHTING: warm low-sun key, dust-bounce fill, sun-flare practicals. ENVIRONMENT: speeding convoy, rising guards, boiling dust. IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Keep the image immaculately clean and premium with clear figure-to-ground separation so the hero reads first; honour the concept's intended dominant surface and full-frame coverage, and do NOT reveal unwanted structural edges, seams, side-walls or empty dead space that break the intended composition. Photoreal, award-winning editorial finish — no AI artifacts, plastic or waxy skin, dead eyes, malformed hands or unfinished backgrounds.

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

LENS: 85mm-equivalent, f/2.8. PHYSICS: the shower of sparks and embers defies the slipstream, sweeping up and organizing into a vast phoenix of fire that spreads and beats its wings once over the convoy, holding its form, then settles into drifting embers; sparks stream off the wings. SUBJECT + OBJECT ACTION (timecoded): 0.0–2.0s as Aira sets from the kick, the sparks and embers off the buckled truck begin to gather and sweep upward (embers lifting); 2.0–4.0s the embers sweep up into a vast fire-phoenix that spreads its wings over the whole convoy and beats once across the crimson sky, Aira looking up in awe, her scarf lowering; 4.0–6.0s the phoenix settles into drifting embers over the convoy, glowing motes streaming down. EXPRESSION TRANSITION: into into — strained effort releases into parted-lip awe at 2.4s as the phoenix rises, blooming into a wild triumphant grin by 4.8s. LIGHTING: self-luminous fire-phoenix up-lighting her face and the convoy, glowing rim. ENVIRONMENT: the convoy crowned by a phoenix of fire. AUDIO (Block A): the engines dropping under a deep rising fire-whoosh and a single great wing-beat from 2.0s, a soft triumphant exhale at 4.8s; score blooms the full choir-brass-and-strings theme over a riser — the peak of the whole set. DURATION: 6.0s. FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps, 180°, silky blur on the rise, crisp fire-phoenix. LIP-SYNC: none; a breath of wonder. NEGATIVE: no gore, no cut, no second move, no cartoonish glow, no warped face, no text.
